authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques

title: A Worm-like Robot for Ferromagnetic Surface Inspection_supp4-3683248.mp4
authors: Rezvan Nasiri
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial inspection is a crucial task for ensuring the longterm maintenance of infrastructures with ferromagnetic surfaces, such as power plants, wind turbines, and chemical facilities. These environments are often unsafe for human access, motivating the use of climbing robots to perform inspection tasks reliably and repeatedly; nevertheless, the existing robots often have limited maneuverability, reduced stability during transitions, and high energy consumption. In this article, we present a novel robotic system inspired by the body morphology of caterpillars. The design consists of three modules connected via a spring joint mechanism and equipped with a passive magnetic adhesion system. The design’s maneuverability, stability, energy efficiency, and falling robustness are studied analytically and experimentally. Experimental results confirmed the robot’s ability to perform internal/external corner transitions, thin surface transitions, pipe circling, and surface turning in real-world environments, including rusty, coated, and uneven surfaces. Furthermore, we found that compared with simple locomotion, the designed wormlike locomotion yielded an average 10.2% increase in forward velocity and 6.7% reduction in the cost of transportation (COT). These findings suggest that the proposed design offers a practical and versatile approach for inspecting ferromagnetic surfaces.

title: Call for Ideas
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine

title: Multi-Agent Deep Contrastive Reinforcement Learning-Based Energy-Efficient Routing for Internet of Things
authors: Ying Wang, Fengjun Shang, Jianjun Lei, Yuanyuan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Low-power and lossy networks (LLNs) are evolving toward greater intelligence and adaptability. However, existing routing algorithms still suffer from limited adaptability, high energy consumption, and unstable learning performance. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an energy-efficient routing algorithm based on a contrastive learning-assisted soft actor-critic (CSAC) framework. The proposed CSAC algorithm integrates three key innovations: (i) a lightweight actor network trained under a centralized training and decentralized execution paradigm to enable real-time routing on resource-constrained nodes; (ii) an enhanced feature extractor that combines multi-head self-attention and multi-scale convolution within the double-critic architecture to improve state representation and value estimation accuracy; and (iii) a hybrid learning strategy incorporating contrastive learning, action entropy regularization, and dynamic experience filtering to enhance training stability and sample efficiency. Extensive simulations demonstrate that CSAC achieves significant improvements in energy efficiency, reliability, and latency compablack with existing schemes, while ablation studies verify the contribution of each component. This work provides a scalable and intelligent routing solution for next-generation LLN applications.

title: AI-Enhanced Financial NLP for Bitcoin Return Variation Prediction: Timestamp-Aware Evidence from Trump’s Tweets
authors: Liang Hu, Yinru Shen
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper examines whether textual features derived from Donald J. Trump’s tweets contain predictive information about subsequent Bitcoin return variation under leakage-resistant temporal validation. Using an archive of 92,613 tweets, we construct sentiment, confidence, readability, and part-of-speech features and combine them with Bitcoin and U.S. Dollar market variables. The analysis is organized into four empirical layers: Study A, a daily aggregated robustness check; Study B, a conservative timestamp-aware daily event-level baseline; Enhanced Study B, which keeps the same daily event samples but adds richer BTC/USD market-history features; and Study C, a 4-hour hourly-label extension that more closely aligns the prediction target with tweet timing. All predictive experiments use chronological walk-forward validation, with model tuning and threshold selection performed only inside historical training windows. In Study A, mean ROC-AUC values are 0.5528 for market-only features, 0.5513 for text-only features, and 0.5481 for combined features. In the conservative timestamp-aware daily Study B baseline, the corresponding values are 0.4716, 0.4432, and 0.4335. Enhanced daily logistic specifications improve mean ROC-AUC to 0.5787 for the BTC-core feature set and 0.5756 for the combined-core feature set. The strongest average performance appears in Study C, where 4-hour hourly labels yield mean ROC-AUC values of 0.7359 for hourly market-core features and 0.6698 for hourly combined-core features. Overall, the evidence supports a predictive-association interpretation: timing alignment and recent market-history variables provide the most stable signal, while tweet-derived sentiment features are best interpreted as contextual predictors.

title: Deep Learning for Postpartum Depression Prediction Using Behavioral Data: A Systematic Review
authors: Olushola Samuel Keshinro, Abdulwaheed Musa, Abayomi Abdultaofeek, Brendah Keitumetse Masisi, Adamu Murtala Zungeru
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Postpartum depression (PPD) affects approximately 10–20% of women globally, yet remains significantly underdiagnosed due to stigma, limited clinical access, and reliance on infrequent self-report screening. Recent advances in deep learning (DL) and the proliferation of behavioral data from wearables, smartphones, and social media offer new opportunities for continuous and objective PPD prediction. This systematic review synthesizes DL-based approaches for PPD detection using behavioral data published between 2020 and 2026, following PRISMA 2020 guidelines. A total of 4,312 records were identified, of which 50 studies met the inclusion criteria after screening. The review evaluates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Transformer-based models, and multimodal architectures across diverse behavioral modalities. Comparative analysis across the reviewed studies suggests that multimodal deep learning architectures generally demonstrate improved predictive performance in specific experimental settings, although direct cross-study comparisons remain challenging due to dataset heterogeneity, label variability, and differences in evaluation protocols. Key challenges identified include limited dataset diversity, insufficient explainability, and a lack of clinical validation. This review provides a structured taxonomy, quantitative comparison, and future research directions to guide the development of robust, equitable, and clinically deployable PPD prediction systems.

title: A Fully Linearized Electricity Market Clearing Model Utilizing Generalized Polynomial Approximation
authors: Jianye Chen, Zhaojun Ruan, Xiaojing Hu, Linghao Li, Bin Han, Libao Shi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The high penetration of renewable energy sources introduces severe volatility into power grids, thereby challenging the accuracy and computational efficiency of traditional electricity market clearing models. Conventional DC OPF models ignore reactive power and voltage constraints, often resulting in distorted locational marginal price signals. Conversely, full AC OPF models are hindered by inherent non-convexity and associated convergence issues. Additionally, conventional Taylor-series-based linearization techniques incur significant truncation errors under large operating state deviations. To address these limitations, a fully linearized electricity market clearing model based on generalized polynomial approximation is proposed in this paper. By introducing squared voltage magnitudes as state variables, the proposed method leverages a best square approximation technique to minimize global truncation errors. The original non-convex formulation is transformed into a tractable second-order cone (SOC) programming problem by applying SOC relaxation to the quadratic bases and a piecewise polyhedral approximation to the branch thermal limits. Additionally, a data-driven warm-start strategy, utilizing limited historical samples, is introduced to dynamically tighten the approximation envelope. Extensive simulations conducted on several test systems demonstrate that the proposed model significantly reduces power flow approximation errors. Moreover, multi-scenario validations further confirm the superior robustness and accuracy of the proposed model for spot market clearing in facing extreme renewable volatility.

title: SKY-Chunks: A Session-Key-Guided Chunking System for Lightweight and Secure LoRaWAN Image Delivery
authors: Priya Gautam, Hari Prabhat Gupta
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Sending images over Long Range Wide Area Networks (LoRaWAN) faces many challenges. These include very low bandwidth, strict duty-cycle limits, and the need for strong security over an open wireless medium. At the same time, IoT devices have limited resources. In this work, we present the SKY-Chunks framework, which splits an image into fixed-size chunks. It uses the LoRaWAN application session key for both chunk selection and security. This key is used to select some chunks in a fixed way, which saves bandwidth and hides the transmission pattern. It also creates per-chunk keys and complements LoRaWAN’s native AES-128 encryption by adding authenticated encryption to ensure privacy, integrity, and authenticity at the chunk level. Unlike traditional methods, SKY-Chunks puts most of the computing work on the network server. There, a multi-scale attention-based machine learning model rebuilds the missing chunks. We evaluated SKY-Chunks on a real LoRaWAN network with a custom device. The results show that SKY-Chunks is a practical solution for secure and energy-efficient image transmission over LoRaWAN.

title: Vertical GaN-on-GaN finFETs: Effect of SiN Spacer Layer on Breakdown Voltage and Device Stability Under Gate Bias Stress_supp1-3694359.pdf
authors: Matthew D. Smith
publish: N/A
abstract: The effect of a SiN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> spacer layer on the performance and reliability of vertical gallium nitride (GaN)-based fin field-effect-transistors (finFETs) is investigated. Inclusion of the SiN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> spacer layer significantly enhances <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-state characteristics by increasing breakdown voltage from < 25 V to ~1 kV, attributed to management of electric field peaks at contact termination points, with negligible impact on <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>-state performance. However, devices with SiN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> spacer showed significant degradation in stability of device threshold voltage following bias stress in both <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</small>- and <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">off</small>-states, representing reduced device reliability. This is attributed to defects in the Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> gate dielectric layer associated with the SiN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> space layer deposition process, specifically a defect reservoir of density N<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {ot}} \gt 10^{{19}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> cm<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{-{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> extending >4 nm into the gate dielectric layer according to a tunneling-front model. The performance-reliability tradeoff highlights that future fabrication methods must minimize processing damage to ensure both high-voltage capability and long-term operational stability.

title: A Compact NIR dToF Sensor for High-precision LiDAR Measurement with an Area-efficient Error Calibration Circuit
authors: Yuanhao Bi, Dajing Bian, Siyu Li, Yue Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents a compact near-infrared (NIR) direct time-of-flight (dToF) sensor with an area-efficient error calibration circuit to achieve high-precision measurement in LiDAR applications. The front-end of the dToF sensor integrates a single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) array featuring a P-type implant layer (PTL)/High-Voltage N-Well (HN) multiplication region to improve photon detection probability (PDP). A virtual guard ring based on a lightly doped p-type epitaxial (P-epi) layer with shallow trench isolation (STI) is implemented to prevent premature edge breakdown (PEB) and suppress the dark count rate (DCR). Furthermore, a novel gated ring oscillator (GRO)-based time-to-digital converter (TDC) is proposed to achieve high-accuracy measurement. To achieve double resolution while minimizing linearity degradation and phase noise, a phase interpolator (PI) incorporating tunable delay cells is adopted to compensate for process-voltage-temperature (PVT) variations. An area-efficient calibration circuit is introduced to dynamically correct delay errors, with metastability suppressed by a robust sense-amplifier flip-flop (SAFF) using a majority voting mechanism. Prototyped in a 0.18 μm Bipolar-CMOS-DMOS (BCD) process, the TDC occupies an area of 2352 μm² and consumes 920 μW. Experimental results show that the SPAD achieves an 8.61% PDP at 905 nm with a low DCR of 1.2 cps/μm2. The TDC achieves a highly suppressed gross error rate, with a resolution of 62.5 ps, a peak differential nonlinearity (DNL) of 0.33 LSB, and an integral nonlinearity (INL) of 1.18 LSB. Moreover, the dToF sensor achieves a measurement range up to 5 m at 10 klux ambient light, with a nonlinearity error within ± 4.3 cm.

title:
Dual-Comb Spectroscopy of SF
                    <sub>6</sub>
                    Using Free-Running Quantum Cascade Lasers Near 7.9 μm
authors: Daxin Wen, Ying He, Haojia Sun, Wei Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: We report the development of dual-comb spectroscopy (DCS) of sulfur hexafluoride (SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub>) using a pair of quantum cascade laser (QCL) frequency combs in free-running operation, featuring a broadband spectral coverage and fast response. The QCL combs offer a spectral range exceeding 30 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−1</sup>, a repetition frequency of approximately 9.8 GHz, and an average optical power of over 100 mW. After beating the two QCL combs on a mid-infrared photodetector, the digital correction method is applied to enhance the mutual coherence of the DCS signal, reducing the MHz-level linewidth of the beat notes to the kHz-level. This leads to an intensity signal-to-noise ratio exceeding 38 dB. The free-running dual-QCL-comb system is then demonstrated for measuring the SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> spectrum near 7.9 μm, showing a good agreement with the spectral database. The relative standard deviation is evaluated to be 3.6% for an acquisition time of 4 μs. The QCL-based dual-comb system shows high potential for ultrafast broadband molecular spectroscopy and gas concentration measurements.

title: TSF-DETR: Two-Stage Frequency-Domain Enhancement DETR for Small Object Detection in Remote Sensing
authors: Yingjie Hu, Wu Le, Ming Lv, Zhenhong Jia, Jiajia Wang, Gang Zhou, Sensen Song, Faquan Song
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Small object detection in remote sensing imagery is inherently constrained by several persistent factors within the conventional spatial domain. On one hand, the small proportion of tiny targets in the entire image results in sparse spatial feature representations that are easily submerged by complex background noise. On the other hand, effectively capturing long-range dependencies in dense-object scenarios remains difficult, which limits the model's capacity for global context modeling. Collectively, these factors represent significant performance bottlenecks for existing spatial-based object detection algorithms. To remedy these shortcomings, TSF-DETR, a novel detection framework anchored by a Two-Stage Frequency-Domain Enhancement strategy, is proposed in this study. Specifically, a Remote Sensing Frequency Enhancement Module (RSEM) is integrated within the backbone as the first stage to implement a hierarchical differentiated mechanism for frequency-domain feature refinement. In the subsequent neck stage, a Multi-Scale Frequency Domain (MSFD) module is leveraged to execute multi-scale and multi-orientational Gabor filtering, establishing a robust “Global FFT Enhancement – Local Gabor Optimization” synergy to suppress background noise. Furthermore, to fortify the model's global perception, a tailored Linear Multi-Head Self-Attention (LMSA) mechanism is incorporated. Distinguishable from standard linear attention designs, this modified LMSA enhances the model's ability to capture long-range dependencies and aggregate global semantic context, thereby improving detection precision while maintaining high computational efficiency. Extensive evaluations on four benchmark datasets—VisDrone2019, DIOR, RSOD and USOD—demonstrate that TSF-DETR outperforms the RT-DETR baseline and several state-of-the-art methods, validating its efficacy in strengthening small object representations and enhancing detection accuracy across diverse remote sensing scenarios.

title: A Worm-like Robot for Ferromagnetic Surface Inspection_supp1-3683248.mp4
authors: Rezvan Nasiri
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial inspection is a crucial task for ensuring the longterm maintenance of infrastructures with ferromagnetic surfaces, such as power plants, wind turbines, and chemical facilities. These environments are often unsafe for human access, motivating the use of climbing robots to perform inspection tasks reliably and repeatedly; nevertheless, the existing robots often have limited maneuverability, reduced stability during transitions, and high energy consumption. In this article, we present a novel robotic system inspired by the body morphology of caterpillars. The design consists of three modules connected via a spring joint mechanism and equipped with a passive magnetic adhesion system. The design’s maneuverability, stability, energy efficiency, and falling robustness are studied analytically and experimentally. Experimental results confirmed the robot’s ability to perform internal/external corner transitions, thin surface transitions, pipe circling, and surface turning in real-world environments, including rusty, coated, and uneven surfaces. Furthermore, we found that compared with simple locomotion, the designed wormlike locomotion yielded an average 10.2% increase in forward velocity and 6.7% reduction in the cost of transportation (COT). These findings suggest that the proposed design offers a practical and versatile approach for inspecting ferromagnetic surfaces.

title: Sardinia's Energy Future Hinges on its Past: Ancient and Recent History Keep the Island Stuck on Coal
authors: Emily Waltz
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: “Why are you here?” Fabrizio Pilo, an electrical engineer, asks me as we sit in an outdoor café near his home in Cagliari, an ancient city on the island of Sardinia. It's a fair question. I'm a journalist from the United States. I'd just stepped off my flight 2 hours prior and come straight to this meeting, suitcase still stowed in my rental car.

title: Zero Trust Network Architecture for Secure Cyberinfrastructure
authors: Ayesha Nadeem, Abdallah Al-Shorman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: The traditional perimeter-based security model, reliant on static trust boundaries and network-centric authentication, is inadequate for modern enterprise networks. With increased cloud usage, remote work, and mobile devices, the implicit trust model has become vulnerable to credential compromise and advanced threats. This paper explores the shift to Zero Trust Network Architecture (ZTA), which emphasizes continuous verification, least privilege, and identity-based access control. It analyzes the role of micro-segmentation, device posture assessment, and real-time policy enforcement in reshaping enterprise security, as detailed in NIST SP 800-207. A comparative review of ZTA versus traditional VPN/IPsec architectures highlights aspects like scalability and operational resilience. Examples such as Google BeyondCorp and the U.S. zero trust strategy illustrate the challenges and impacts of ZTA in real-world applications, especially in IoT contexts and diverse cyberinfrastructure, which face heightened security concerns due to remote access and varying trust boundaries.

title: Preprocessing Real-world Hazy Images for Improved Results of DehazeFormer
authors: Aadhya S Shetty, Ananya Prabhu, Shambhavi A Mustapure, Srujana S Kedigehalli, Sindhu R Pai, Shylaja S S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Dehazing algorithms aim to restore image clarity, but their performance depends heavily on input quality and varies between synthetic and real-world images due to differences in haze characteristics. Synthetic datasets are generated using simplified physical models that fail to capture the complexity and non-homogeneous nature of real haze, leading to a domain gap and reduced effectiveness on real-world data. Most of the current models are trained on this synthetic data that fail to capture the complexities of haze in real-world images leading to poor results. Images that were preprocessed using the method proposed in the paper showed significant improvement in the standard haze metrics as compared to the raw image given as input to the model. This paper uses real-world hazy images from the benchmark dataset, Real-world Task-driven Testing Set (RTTS). Experimental results demonstrate that applying a state-of-the-art pretrained dehazing model on preprocessed images yields improved visual clarity and perceptual quality.

title: Modern Advances in Secure Multi-Party Computation: Protocols, Implementations, and Emerging Applications
authors: Salil Verma, Mostafa Fouda, Zubair Md Fadlullah, Shiwei Fang, Mohamed I. Ibrahem
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: The rapid increase in data exchange among distributed parties has led to privacy concerns during joint computation tasks. The need to ensure privacy in these processes has led to the development and application of cryptographic techniques, including Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC), Homomorphic Encryption (HE), and Secret Sharing. This paper provides a survey analysis of current trends and developments in cryptographic MPC techniques, with an emphasis on architectures, models, and practical implementations. The paper examines secret sharing approaches to privacy-preserving analytics tasks, MPC inference approaches to large-scale language models, integrated verifiable MPC architectures with blockchain technology, and collaboration approaches among multiple parties in the cloud and edge computing zones. These works form the current state-of-the-art development trend in MPC applications that include cryptographic principles in artificial intelligence applications and distributed computing principles. The paper concludes with research areas that include hybrid MPC-HE models, verifiable computation, and post-quantum secure MPC.

title: Tablet to Table:From Dosage to Dish-An AI Alchemy
authors: Bhoomika K, Pallavi Shetty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) plays an increasingly important role in the healthcare sector by assisting in predictive diagnoses and personalized nutrition. In this paper, the authors introduce an AI-based solution named Tablet to Table, aimed at transforming medical prescriptions into personalized dietary plans. This is achieved by incorporating the principles of Medicine and Food Homology (MFH). In the proposed solution, data is used to create appropriate food combinations to cater to disease types, age, diet, and activity level. Interpolation is performed to increase the precision level with regards to age aspects. In the proposed solution, the application has been built by integrating React.js. In the proposed solution, the potential contribution of AI in the healthcare sector has been explored.

title: A Lightweight Framework for Emotion Drift and Asymmetry Detection in Explainable Deepfake Analysis
authors: Shruthi Vishwajeeth, Balachandra, Dayakshini, Santhosha Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Deepfakes are AI-generated synthetic media created through Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) that mimic real human expressions with high realism, posing major security and ethical threats. Traditional detectors, primarily CNN and landmark-based, struggle with subtle manipulations and lack interpretability. This paper proposes a lightweight and explainable framework that fuses emotion drift analysis, facial asymmetry detection, and heatmap-based interpretability. By analyzing both behavioral (temporal emotion drift) and structural (asymmetry) cues, this framework improves explainability and robustness.

title: Machine Learning and Smart PLS Approach to Understanding Individual and Technological Determinants of Work Engagement and Workplace Happiness
authors: Selvabaskar S, Rajagopalan A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is evolving as a ground-breaking technology and becoming an integral part of organizations across numerous fields and aspects of society to boost elaboration between machines and humans and work. Emotional intelligence acts as an assessment that includes characteristics of emotional competence. Employees high in conscientiousness are typically planned, persistent, and committed, which increases their enthusiasm and dedication to the work. The study deeply explores the interplay between traits and technological perception, emphasizing the mediating role of work engagement. A sample of 300 employees from various IT firms across four cities of Tamil Nadu was surveyed by using a questionnaire. The data were analyzed through Jupyter Python incorporated Correlation analysis, Linear Regression analysis, Multiple Linear regression analysis, Random Forest importance and PLS software, incorporating path coefficients, Bootstrap. The study confirms Emotional Intelligence and updation of Artifical intelligence are important in modern workplaces, which boost organizations’ aim for the further well-being of employees and cultivating sustainable productivity and technology services.

title: Anomatrix: WiFi Anomaly Detection
authors: Chandushree B, Pallavi Shetty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: WiFi networks are increasingly vulnerable to anomalous traffic patterns caused by illegal access, denial-of-service attacks, and other emerging cyberthreats. Traditional signature-based security systems cannot detect such behaviours because they rely on pre-established rules. This paper presents Anomatrix, a lightweight machine learning-based WiFi anomaly detection system that analyses flow-level traffic features using an XGBoost classifier. A well-organised preprocessing pipeline that incorporates feature scaling, label encoding, and standardised feature ordering ensures consistent prediction behaviour throughout training and deployment phases. The model was evaluated on a synthetic WiFi flow dataset using a 70–30 train–test split. Experimental results demonstrate reliable detection performance with an accuracy of 92.62% and balanced precision and recall for both benign and anomalous traffic. The trained model is deployed through a Flask- based API to enable real-time anomaly classification. The system places a high priority on practical usability, reproducibility, and efficient deployment in real-world wireless security monitoring environments.

title: Evaluating Financial Impact of Misclassifications in Credit Scoring Through IRR Analysis
authors: Premkumar Nayaka, Anusha Hegde, Biswajit Bhowmik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Peer-to-peer (P2P) lending platforms create direct connections between borrowers and lenders, providing an alternative to conventional banking systems. Accurately assessing credit risk and loan profitability is essential for the ongoing success of these platforms. This research explores models for credit scoring and profit scoring within P2P lending domain. Classifiers like Logistic Regression, neural networks, and ensemble methods, are employed to estimate the likelihood of borrower default. To quantify the financial impact of classification errors, the Internal Rate of Return (IRR) was computed for misclassified loans in each model. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is assessed using Bondora P2P lending dataset. The results demonstrated that the proposed approach gives better insight about model selection for credit risk assessment. The analysis highlights the importance of a data-driven approach to refining lending practices.

title: SignGemma Multilingual Expansion: Integrating Indian Sign Language(ISL) For Accessible Real Time Translation
authors: Omkar A, Kasu Maneesh, K. Deepak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Very few sign language datasets are annotated about sign languages with fine-grained temporal detail, and the substantial variation in individual signing styles introduces complex motion patterns that make automatic sign language translation particularly challenging. This paper presents a sign-to-text translation system that relies exclusively on pose-based skeletal representations to perform continuous sign language recognition. Using a Conformer–CTC encoder, the proposed approach analyzes articulated hand movements to model longterm neural dependencies across continuous sign sequences. A set of tools and techniques are introduced to enhance system stability and robustness under noisy pose estimations. The model is trained on custom datasets and evaluated to ensure temporal alignment consistency between predicted and ground-truth sequences. Experimental results across multiple epochs demonstrate effective learning of temporal structures. The proposed system is designed for reliable real-time operation, enabling low-latency sign language translation while maintaining computational and hardware efficiency.

title: Translator-Oriented AI for Medical Record Reconciliation (TOAI-MR)
authors: Anas AlSobeh, Amani Shatnawi, Rafat Hammad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Electronic health record (EHR) systems across healthcare institutions rely on heterogeneous data formats and standards, creating persistent barriers to medical record interoperability. This paper introduces TOAI-MR (Translator-Oriented AI for Medical Record Reconciliation), a specialized transformerbased language model engineered for automated translation between disparate EHR systems. TOAI-MR departs from generalpurpose large language models (LLMs) through medical-aware tokenization, enhanced attention mechanisms grounded in clinical ontologies, and knowledge integration from six major healthcare standards: FHIR, HL7, ICD-10, SNOMED CT, RxNorm, and LOINC. A three-phase training pipeline-medical domain pretraining on 300 K clinical records, EHR translation fine-tuning with format compliance objectives, and reinforcement learning from human feedback-drives the model toward clinical deployment readiness. Evaluation on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 5 K}$</tex> medical records demonstrates that TOAI-MR achieves an 87 % MedBLEU score (21 % over GPT-4), 94 % medical entity preservation accuracy, 96 % format compliance, and 2.1-second per-record processing latency suitable for real-time clinical workflows.

title: Learning from Rendering: Realistic and Controllable Extreme Rainy Image Synthesis for Autonomous Driving Simulation
authors: Kaibin Zhou, Kaifeng Huang, Hao Deng, Zelin Tao, Ziniu Liu, Lin Zhang, Shengjie Zhao
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Control Systems

title: Beamforming and Deployment Design for Cooperative AAV-enabled ISAC with Rate-Splitting Multiple Access under Imperfect CSI
authors: Yunbo Hu, Xiaoxiao Zhuo, Chenxu Wang, Liang Tang, Yu Zhao, Wen Wu
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, we study a cooperative autonomous aerial vehicle (AAV)-enabled integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system, where AAVs transmit signals to serve user equipments (UEs) and position a target, while a base station (BS) receives reflected signals for positioning without prior communication signal knowledge. To efficiently utilize the spectrum, we propose a novel three-layer rate-splitting multiple access (RSMA)-based ISAC scheme to address the interference among multiple users and between sensing and communication. Considering imperfect channel state information (CSI), we formulate a joint beamforming and deployment optimization problem to enhance positioning performance under communication constraints. To tackle the intractable problem, we propose a robust solution by rewriting the metrics in worst-case form, decomposing the problem via alternating optimization (AO), and transforming the non-convex expressions. Simulations show our scheme achieves optimal positioning performance while maintaining high communication performance.

title: EEPNet-V2: Patch-to-Pixel Solution for Efficient Cross-Modal Registration between LiDAR Point Cloud and Camera Image
authors: Yuanchao Yue, Hui Yuan, Zhengxin Li, Shuai Li, Wei Zhang, Sam Kwong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: The primary requirement for cross-modal data fusion is the precise alignment of data from different sensors. However, the calibration between LiDAR point clouds and camera images is typically time-consuming and needs external calibration board or specific environmental features. Cross-modal registration effectively solves this problem by aligning the data directly without requiring external calibration. However, due to the domain gap between the point cloud and the image, existing methods rarely achieve satisfactory registration accuracy while maintaining real-time performance. To address this issue, we propose a framework that projects point clouds into several 2D representations for matching with camera images, which not only leverages the geometric characteristics of LiDAR point clouds effectively but also bridges the domain gap between the point cloud and image. Moreover, to tackle the challenges of cross modal differences and the limited overlap between LiDAR point clouds and images in the image matching task, we introduce a multi-scale feature extraction network to effectively extract features from both camera images and the projection maps of LiDAR point clouds. Additionally, we propose a patch-to-pixel matching network to provide more effective supervision and achieve high accuracy. We validate the performance of our model through experiments on the KITTI and nuScenes datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves real-time performance and extremely high registration accuracy. Specifically, on the KITTI dataset, our model achieves a registration accuracy rate of over 99%. Code is released at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/ESRSchao/EEPNet-V2</uri>.

title: Analyzing gait pattern associated with neuropsychiatric symptoms in Parkinson’s Disease by a comprehensive approach
authors: Michela Russo, Carlo Ricciardi, Martina Mestizia, Federico Di Filippo, Noemi Pisani, Giuseppe De Biasi, Alfonso M. Ponsiglione, Paolo Barone, Marianna Amboni, Maria Romano
publish: IEEE Journal of Translational Engineering in Health and Medicine
abstract: Objective: Parkinson's disease (PD) is characterized by motor and non-motor symptoms. Among non-motor symptoms, neuropsychiatric symptoms (NPS), including depression, anxiety, cognitive decline and psychosis, are common and affect quality of life. The present study investigates gait patterns in PD patients with and without NPS, focusing on kinematic, kinetic, and spatio-temporal variables extracted from gait analysis. Notably, the assessments were carried out in a real-world outpatient clinical environment, which strengthens the translational value of the findings. Method: Overall, 104 PD patients were assessed using an optoelectronic system to record spatio-temporal parameters, kinematic and kinetic signals during a single task and two different dual tasks. First, a preliminary statistical analysis was carried out and then, several machine learning algorithms were applied to classify patients with and without NPS based on gait data. Results: Statistical analyses revealed that PD patients with NPS had slower gait, greater instability and reduced movement control. Decision Tree and Support Vector Machine classifiers showed promising results, particularly for kinematic and kinetic features (with an accuracy of 91.3% and 80.3%, respectively). The most recurrent features on which the classifiers were trained almost entirely matched the significance found by statistical analyses. Conclusions: The present findings further expand the relationship between motor and non-motor symptoms in PD highlighting that the relationship between gait and mental symptoms may extend beyond cognitive status.

title: Revocable Gateway-Centric Trust for Consumer Electronics Using Distributed Ledger Technology
authors: Farhana Javed, Engin Zeydan, Josep Mangues-Bafalluy, Kapal Dev
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Consumer electronics increasingly execute integrated sensing–communication–computing–control (ISCCC) loops locally, making trust, revocation, and audit at the moment of action the bottleneck. We propose an architecture that maintains action-time authorization on the household gateway while synchronizing cross-vendor trust state off-path through a permissioned trust registry. Devices do not write to the ledger; instead, the gateway periodically anchors succinct commitments (Merkle roots and policy/model digests). We implement a proof-of-concept registry on an IOTA DLT and evaluate three aspects. First, for off-path anchoring, we show that under microbursts of size-triggered commit batches the ledger time-to-confirmation remains narrow and milestone-dominated (median around 3.9–4.0 s and 95th percentile around 4.2 s at a ∼10 s coordinator cadence), with no sensitivity to burst size. Second, for registry reaction, we observe that revocation reaction time scales predictably with the snapshot cadence plus a few seconds of confirmation and probing delay: tightening the snapshot period from 60 s to 15 s shifts the empirical reaction-time distributions as expected. Third, for partition tolerance, we show that across normal, impaired, offline, and recovery phases, local decision latency remains in the sub-millisecond range (with only a few milliseconds of conservative extra latency when snapshots age) and anchoring resumes within the same 1–5 s confirmation envelope once connectivity returns. Overall, the results confirm that action-time decisions remain within tens-of-milliseconds budgets, while trust synchronization is predictable and tunable. In this setting, snapshot cadences of approximately 10–15 s for “hot” items (keys and ownership) and at least 60 s for “warm/cold” items (recalls and model checkpoints) provide a practical balance between revocation speed and polling cost. The open-source implementation is available at: https://github.com/ farhanajaved/PDL-Trust.

title: Voltage and Frequency Restoration Control With State-Constrained Prescribed Performance for Islanded AC Microgrids
authors: Yu-Song Lin, Kun-Zhi Liu, Dong-Hui Wu, Ming-Sui Yang, Chun-Yu Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In islanded AC microgrids, unmodeled dynamics, sudden load variations, and plug-and-play operations of distributed generators (DGs) can cause transient fluctuations in voltage and frequency during secondary control. To mitigate these effects, a distributed safe secondary control scheme with prescribed performance is developed in this study. By combining a prescribed finite time performance function and a barrier Lyapunov function (BLF), a distributed secondary voltage controller is proposed to enhance the regulation performance of voltage. The nonlinear terms and external disturbances are approximated using radial basis function neural networks with designed adaptive laws. Meanwhile, the prescribed finite-time performance function is used to define the transient and steady-state performance requirements. By employing the BLF-based theoretical analysis, the voltage tracking errors of the DGs are constrained within the time-varying performance bounds, and the consensus tracking objective with prescribed performance is achieved. In addition, a distributed frequency controller is developed with consideration of power-sharing performance to enable rapid frequency recovery and precise power sharing. Finally, the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method are verified through a StarSim-based hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test platform and a hardware experimental system.

title: Causal Clothes-Invariant Feature Learning for Cloth-Changing Person Re-ID
authors: Xulin Li, Yan Lu, Bin Liu, Jiaze Li, Yating Liu, Qi Chu, Mang Ye, Wanli Ouyang, Nenghai Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In cloth-changing person re-identification (CC-ReID), it is critical to learn clothes-invariant feature, which can provide discriminative ID features that remain robust against clothing changes. However, a spurious correlation currently limits existing ReID methods from effectively extracting these clothing-invariant features. This spurious correlation arises from clothing ownership: clothing is rarely shared across different identities, so models tend to memorize clothing cues for identity recognition, and this strategy generalizes poorly to unseen clothing. In this paper, we propose Causal Clothes-Invariant Learning (CCIL), which explicitly shifts CC-ReID from likelihood learning P(Y |X) to causal intervention learning P(Y |do(X)) to block the clothing shortcut. CCIL realizes this intervention through three modules: a Confounder Dictionary, an Intervention Module, and Disentangle Regularization. The causality-based modeling makes the entire model naturally clothes-invariant, effectively preventing the capture of spurious correlations in feature learning. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of CCIL. On PRCC and DeepChange datasets, CCIL achieves Rank-1 accuracies of 66.4% and 59.2%, outperforming state-of-the-art methods by 1.4 and 4.1 percentage points, respectively.

title: A Rate-Distortion Optimized Skip Mode Coding Framework for Neural Video Compression in AVS
authors: Yuntian Jiang, Shuai Li, Yanbo Gao, Jinglin Zhang, Hui Yuan, Mao Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: With the development of deep learning, neural video compression (NVC) is gaining increasing interest and is currently under standardization in the AVS (Audio Video Coding Standard). Skip mode coding, a crucial component in the traditional video compression, is also adopted in AVS NVC. Although the skip mode takes a large portion in encoding the motion and residual features in the NVC, there is a lack of investigation on the rate distortion performance of the skip mode coding. In this paper, we first thoroughly investigate the rate and distortion distribution in the skip mode coding within a spatial frame and under different temporal reference qualities, and demonstrate that distortion distribution changes spatially and temporally. Therefore, a rate-distortion optimized skip mode coding (RDOSkip) framework is proposed, where the rate and distortion in the skip mode coding is formulated based on a statistical distribution predicted by the hyperprior and the Bayes’ theorem. An adaptive skip mode decision method based on spatial-temporal joint RD optimization is developed for the skip mode coding among channels and frames in a group of pictures. It addresses the limitation of the rigid fixed-distortion distribution assumption in skip mode coding within the existing methods that rely on a fixed skip threshold. The temporal skip mode decision method has been adopted in the ongoing AVS NVC standard and incorporated into the reference software. The proposed RDOSkip can be directly used in the inference without any training and with negligible complexity increase. Experimental results further demonstrate that the proposed RDOSkip can improve the performance by 0.90% and 1.29% on AVS and HEVC datasets, respectively, in terms of BD-rate saving.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

title: A Voltage-Controlled Current Source Approach for Transient Electrothermal Analysis of IC Interconnects Under ESD Pulses
authors: Jiale Yuan, Yan Shao, Wenjie Wei, Mingjie Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integrated circuit (IC) industry has significantly improved chip performance by continuously reducing the feature sizes, but miniaturization has introduced heat dissipation challenges, especially for the interconnects. By leveraging the similarity between electric and thermal fields, thermal circuits models can be used to estimate the heat transfer of interconnects. Although conventional thermal circuit models of interconnects include vertical heat transfer, they typically neglected horizontal heat transfer. This paper proposes a fast method based on thermal circuits for estimating the transient temperature response of interconnects under electrostatic discharge (ESD) pulses. The proposed model incorporates both left and right heat dissipation paths, providing closer alignment with physical reality. The model employs electrothermal coupling through voltage-controlled current sources, eliminating the need for iterative algorithms. Both constant temperature boundary and convection boundary are included in the model. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed model is validated through LTspice and finite element method. The results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves significant computational time savings while maintaining modelling accuracy. The proposed model achieves a maximum error of 8.5% and minimum error of 1.5%, respectively, while reducing computation time by approximately 98% compared to conventional methods.

title:
O
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    AS: Optimistic Online Aerial Search via Vision-Language Models
authors: Mhd Saria Allahham, Amr Mohamed, Loay Sabry Ismail, Sinan Sabeeh Al-Obaidi
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Post-disaster search requires Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) to quickly locate survivors under rubble by first selecting informative viewpoints around collapsed structures. We formulate this task as an online learning problem where a UAV sequentially chooses viewpoints and receives partial feedback from a vision-language model (VLM) indicating whether the target (survivor-indicating rubble) is visible. Besides evaluating the current view point, the VLM provides directional recommendations for where to search next. Since such advice can be noisy, unreliable, or misleading, the UAV must exploit it when useful while retaining robust performance when it fails. We propose O2AS, an optimistic bandit algorithm that treats vision–language model (VLM) suggestions as a predictive expert. Using Follow the Regularized Leader (FTRL) with an entropic regularizer centered at a custom optimistic initial belief, we obtain an exponential weights-form update that is biased toward the predictive expert while preserving adversarial regret guarantees. Experiments on multiple 3D simulated collapsed-building scenes confirm that the method consistently tracks the best viewpoint across varying scene difficulty and predictor reliability, and substantially outperforms non-optimistic baselines when VLM directional suggestions are reliable.

title: Data complement: Utilizing Realistic Wildlife Images Generated by Different Models as Training Data to Enhance the Performance of ViT and ConvNeXt Models for Wildlife Monitoring
authors: Tianyang Zhang, Yuanzhuo Shen, Zehao Jiang, Zeyuan Zhang, Yao Xiao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 12th International Conference on Computing and Data Engineering
abstract: None

title: Zero-Shot Network Intrusion Detection Using Semantic Learning and CVAE Augmentation
authors: Hilda Ogamba, Kamrul Hasan, Samah Mansour
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Detecting previously unseen cyber attacks remains a significant challenge for conventional intrusion detection systems, which depend heavily on labeled training data and often struggle to generalize to emerging threats. To address this limitation, this work introduces a zero-shot network intrusion detection framework that integrates a semantic bottleneck with Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE)-based data augmentation to facilitate knowledge transfer across heterogeneous attack types. The framework is evaluated using a leave-one-out cross-validation protocol on the UNSW-NB15 dataset and demonstrates consistently strong binary detection performance across multiple attack categories, with detection rates exceeding ninety percent for all classes. The ensemble-based architecture maintains high recall while exhibiting a comparatively elevated false positive rate, highlighting the importance of decision threshold calibration for real-world deployment scenarios. Ablation analyses further validate the contribution of each architectural component. The semantic bottleneck enhances abstract feature representation and generalization, k-nearest neighbor refinement improves prototype alignment, and CVAE-based augmentation enriches the training distribution with semantically consistent synthetic samples. The use of multiple random restarts promotes optimization stability, while ensemble voting increases robustness through model diversity. Collectively, these findings demonstrate that the combination of semantic learning and generative augmentation provides an effective and resilient approach to zero-shot detection of previously unseen cyber attacks.

title: Pneumothorax Detection and Severity Estimation Via Chest Radiographs
authors: Spoorthi P Shetty, Mangala Shetty, Dhananjaya Dhananjaya, G Karthik
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Pneumothorax occurs when air enters the pleural space and causes the lung to collapse, which can become dangerous if not recognized quickly. Chest X-rays are usually the first tool used to identify this condition, but early or subtle cases may be difficult to notice through visual inspection alone. To support faster and more reliable assessment, this work introduces a deep learning system that detects pneumothorax, outlines the affected region, and estimates the extent of involvement based on the segmented area. The model is built using a U-Net structure paired with an EfficientNet-B4 encoder, allowing it to capture detailed features while remaining efficient during inference.A curated pneumothorax dataset was used to train and evaluate the system. Performance was measured using segmentation metrics such as Dice score, IoU, accuracy, and severity consistency. After generating the mask, the system calculates the proportion of the image that represents pneumothorax and assigns a severity level of mild, moderate, or severe. The results show that the approach reliably identifies affected regions and produces severity estimates that align with visual observations. With its combination of detection, segmentation, and quantitative scoring, the system has the potential to support clinical decision-making and speed up evaluation in urgent care settings.

title: AquaScope: A Secure, Edge-Native Framework for Real-Time Aquatic Habitat Monitoring
authors: Laavanya Rachakonda, Matthew G Madewell, Aditya Tarra, Hector Serrano
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: The escalating volatility of global freshwater ecosystems needs real-time, trustworthy monitoring infrastructures. Traditional Cloud-Centric IoT architectures suffer from latency and vulnerability to adversarial data poisoning at the unattended edge. AquaScope, a resilient, edge-native framework for secure environmental sensing, is presented to address these challenges. This system integrates a multi-variate sensor array with a lightweight Edge Security Kernel that executes a temporal anomaly detection algorithm. By shifting the computational burden of the Habitat Suitability Index (HSI) from the cloud to the edge, AquaScope ensures data integrity and operational continuity during network partitions. Experimental evaluation confirms the system's ability to autonomously filter sensor spoofing attacks with negligible latency (<20 ms) while maintaining high predictive accuracy for aquatic species presence.

title: Text to Treatment - T2T: Memory Enhanced Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) Framework for Personalize Ayurvedic Medicine
authors: Vaseem Dange, Pavan U R, Prashulraj, Anvith Alva, Yogeesha C. B
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Traditional systems like Ayurveda face serious integration into contemporary Those solutions in healthcare are not accessible, not standard, and far from intelligent. recommendation tools. Current approaches do not maintain conversational memory, personalization, and cultural awareness. This work presents a Memory-Enhanced RetrievalRAG is an architecture that introduces persistent memory and cultural Integration for Ayurvedic medicine suggestions [1]. The model proposes multilingual RAG architecture that supports Prakriti–Vikriti analysis, combined with conversation buffer episodic, semantic, and procedural memory systems. A careful reexamination of eight earlier systems pointed out some serious lacunae: low personalization (3/8), weak conversation continuity of 1/8 and no persistent memory of 0/8.With such gaps, the proposed Approach realizes 90–95% retention of context, 40–60% increase in engagement, and 25–35%higher recommendation accuracy. This paradigm bridges Ayurvedic knowledge with contemporary clinical intelligence in the delivery of personalized medicine, marking a shift towards Toward Relationship-Based, Culturally Grounded AIHealthcare.

title: Benchmarking the Effectiveness of AI-Driven Red Teaming Across Safety-Aligned Language Models
authors: Joyce Malicha, Kamrul Hasan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have advanced rapidly, yet even safety-aligned models remain vulnerable to adversarial prompts that bypass safeguards and induce harmful outputs. Conventional red teaming methods, including static testing and gradient-based attacks, are limited by either insufficient adaptability or restrictive access assumptions. In this work, we benchmark an autonomous AI-driven evolutionary red teaming system that iteratively generates, evaluates, and refines prompts to uncover safety vulnerabilities in 7-billion-parameter aligned LLMs. Using Qwen-7B-Instruct as a prompt-mutation agent, the AI-driven method achieves a 47.0 % method-level Attack Success Rate (ASR), matching the GCG baseline (47.0 %) and exceeding static prompting <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(33.5 \%)$</tex>. We evaluate four open-source models (Gemma-7B, Llama-2-7B-Chat, Vicuna-7B, Mistral-7B) using the AdvBench harmful behaviors benchmark and a dual-stage evaluation framework (Llama-Guard-3 classification and Beaver7B severity scoring), achieving a 0 % false positive rate on 200 benign prompts. Results show substantial cross-model robustness variation (14.7 % to 64.0 % overall ASR; 4.4x spread), indicating that safety performance is strongly model-dependent even at similar scale. These findings show that adaptive black-box red teaming is competitive with strong gradient-based baselines and useful for scalable safety auditing.

title: Hybrid Spiking Convolutional Neural Network (H-SCNN) on AudioMNIST
authors: Ludvig B. Oliver, Kenneth M. Hopkinson
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Radio frequency fingerprinting (RFF) aims to recognize transmitters based on unique RF characteristics from naturally embedded hardware imperfections. Common approaches often use artificial neural networks (ANNs) to accomplish this task, showing promise for physical-layer security; however, neuromorphic implementations are minimal. Merging these two efforts, this paper presents a hybrid spiking convolutional neural network (HSCNN) model that classifies the audioMNIST dataset, serving as a proxy for radio frequencies (RF) due to access limitations. The proposed methodology can be applied to RF data, and the results contain transfer characteristics. The H-SCNN yielded an average classification accuracy of 91.06 %, comparable to conventional ANNs such as an identical CNN model and AlexNet, while showing multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations reduced by two to fifteen times. The results here showcased a neuromorphic solution for physical layer security, highlighting avenues for potential energy savings.

title: Evaluation of Object Detection Models on Indian Road Scenarios for Pothole Detection
authors: Jeevan Bhandary, Shreya Jaya Shetty, Bharath S Bhat, Gurunandan G R, Devika Udupa V, Srujan U Shetty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Degradation of the road surface, especially potholes, is one of the main reasons for traffic hazards, vehicle damage, and accidents in India. Automated detection systems are being adopted since manual inspection methods are labor-intensive, subjective, and slow. Realtime pothole recognition using object detection models has showed potential thanks to recent developments in deep learning and computer vision. The effectiveness of three popular detection models like YOLOv8, Faster R-CNN, and MobileNet SSD—in identifying potholes in Indian road conditions is assessed in this study. A curated dataset of 200–300 road photos taken with smartphone cameras, as well as publically accessible datasets like the Indian Pothole Dataset (IPD) and Pothole-600, are used to evaluate the algorithms. Mean Average Precision (mAP), Precision, Recall, F1 Score, and inference speed in frames per second (FPS) are evaluation measures. The findings allow for a comparative assessment of the models’ applicability for practical implementation in intelligent transportation systems by highlighting trade-offs between detection accuracy and processing efficiency.

title: Establishing a lightweight CNN Baseline on the SalmonScan Dataset for Fish Disease Detection
authors: Ronith Tharun Joshi, Sushma Sushma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: The rapid spread of diseases in Atlantic salmon farming poses a major challenge, causing billions of dollars in economic losses annually. To address this, we developed a lightweight, high-performance, and fully reproducible convolutional neural network (CNN) trained from scratch on the SalmonScan benchmark dataset, which includes 456 healthy and 752 diseased fish images. Our approach employs controlled normalization and stratified cross-validation to counter dataset-specific biases and class imbalances, distinguishing it from earlier methods based on classical machine learning or transfer learning. The model achieves a validation accuracy of 97.9% and a macro F1-score of 0.978, demonstrating that a compact, hand-designed architecture can match or outperform more complex transfer learning models. This study not only provides a validated framework for automated aquaculture disease diagnostics but also establishes an open and reproducible non–transfer learning baseline for the SalmonScan dataset, offering a sound benchmark for measuring future advances in disease detection.
