title: Federated and Reinforcement Learning for Cyber Defense: A Comprehensive Survey of Emerging Intelligent Security Paradigms
authors: Rishikesh Bajgai, Mohamed I. Ibrahem, Zubair Md Fadlullah, Mostafa Fouda
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: With a continuous increase in the use of digital platforms for day-to-day applications and the rapid rise in the complexity and frequency of cyber threats, intelligent, privacypreserving defense mechanisms are increasingly used across critical domains, such as government, finance, defense, healthcare, and education. Conventional centralized machine learning (ML) methods have been effective to a certain extent. However, they involve sharing sensitive data during model training, thereby posing serious privacy and security risks. On the other hand, federated learning (FL) enables collaborative model development without sharing raw data. This helps ensure confidentiality in a distributed setting. Furthermore, reinforcement learning (RL) learns autonomously and adapts to develop defense strategies that effectively counter evolving cyberattack patterns. We focus on these two ML approaches for the cyber defense application, along with a combination of FL and RL (FL–RL). The paper examines the taxonomy for FL–RL-based cyber defense, system architectures, applications, common attacks, defense strategies, comparative analysis, and future research directions.

title: AI-Driven Liver Disease Prediction: A Machine Learning and LSTM-Based Approach with Clinical Data
authors: Ashwin Shenoy M, Santhosh S, N. Thillaiarasu, Sharanya S Suresh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Liver disease is a critical global health issue, contributing significantly to morbidity and mortality. Early detection is essential for timely clinical intervention. This study aims to develop and evaluate multiple machine learning (ML) models for predicting liver disease using clinical and biochemical patient data. The dataset underwent preprocessing, including normalization, missing value handling, and feature selection to improve model performance. Five ML techniques were compared: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree (DT), K-Means Clustering, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Recurrent Neural Network (RNN). SVM and DT were chosen for their effectiveness with structured data; K-Means for unsupervised clustering to identify hidden patient patterns; and LSTM and RNN to capture temporal trends in patient records. Results showed that LSTM achieved the best performance with 91.2% accuracy, an F1-score of 0.89, and an AUC-ROC of 0.93, demonstrating strong temporal learning capability. RNN followed closely, while DT achieved 85.7% accuracy with high interpretability. SVM also performed well in high-dimensional space. K-Means revealed meaningful patient groupings but lacked predictive strength. This comparative analysis demonstrates that deep learning models, particularly LSTM, are highly effective for liver disease prediction and suggests that hybrid approaches may further enhance diagnostic accuracy and clinical decision-making.

title: Artificial Intelligence Based Prediction of Menopausal Transition Phases from Symptomatology
authors: Manjula Gururaj Rao, Priyanka H, Sthuthi R, Pragathi S Rao, Tarani S Kulkarni, Pranvi P Shetty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Menopause is a critical changeover in a woman’s life, often accompanied by physical and psychological changes that affect overall health. Early identification of menopausal stages can support timely medical guidance and preventive care. This study explores the use of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques to predict menopausal stages based on symptom-related survey data. The model is able to predict 3 stages as peri-menopause, menopause and post-menopause. The collected responses and data set is created based on same. The dataset is preprocessed and analyzed using various classification models to determine the stage of menopause. The ensemble-based learning has shown the result of 86.55% of accuracy. The explainable AI is used to get the better result on the prediction of different stages of the menopause.

title: Human Karyotype Analysis Using Deep Learning with Chatbot Assistance
authors: Vandana B.S, Akiko Hanai, Ashwini. B, Dhruva Shedbalkar, Dhriti Shetty, Dhyanchand, Aashna Mathias
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Automated chromosome identification in karyotype image is essential for clinical diagnosis but this remains challenging due to its structural complexity of metaphase. The proposed system utilizing the YOLOv8m architecture for accurate chromosome analysis, integrated with a diagnostic chatbot by using Natural Language Processing (NLP). The YOLOv8m model was trained on an extensive dataset to classify 24 distinct chromosome classes (autosomes 1-22, X, Y) as well as numerical (trisomy, monosomy) and structural abnormalities(deletion). The overall accuracy of the detection Model, quantified by Mean Average Precision (mAP), is 99.2%. After detecting an abnormality, the NLP-driven chatbot processes and diagnose through its knowledge bases by curating and structuring information from validated cytogenetic databases, scientific literature, and external resources. This chatbot accurately answers the queries human gene system. Proposed system provides users with medical context including syndrome names, associated genes, and phenotypic effects with good accuracy in both detection and clinical interpretation

title: Predicting Student Academic Performance Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Sailaja Pulagam, Reshma Sai Aishwarya Artham, Muhammad Zubair Khan, Belinda Copus
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Accurately predicting student academic performance is a key objective in educational data mining and learning analytics, as early identification of at-risk students enables timely and targeted academic interventions. In this study, we investigate the problem of final grade prediction using the UCI Student Performance dataset and formulate it as a supervised regression task. Four widely adopted regression models-Linear Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Support Vector Regression (SVR)-are implemented and evaluated under a consistent preprocessing and validation framework. Experimental results show that ensemble tree-based models, particularly Random Forest, achieve superior generalization performance, attaining a test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.83 and the lowest root mean squared error (RMSE). Beyond predictive accuracy, this work emphasizes model interpretability by analyzing feature importance to identify the academic and behavioral factors that most strongly influence final outcomes. Prior-period grades emerge as the dominant predictors, while attendance, study time, and previous failures provide additional explanatory power. Rather than introducing a new algorithm, this paper provides an interpretable and deployment-oriented empirical baseline for AI-based academic decision-support systems. The findings demonstrate how transparent machine learning models can support early-warning frameworks within institutional analytics workflows, balancing predictive performance with practical usability.

title: A Real-Time Hardware-In-The-Loop Water SCADA Testbed for Cyber-Physical Research
authors: Tosin Akinsowon, Damilola Oladimeji, Rasheed Amar, Mohamed Baza
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Water facilities increasingly rely on Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems for real-time monitoring and control, making them critical targets for cy-ber-physical attacks. However, the lack of realistic, reproducible experimental platforms limits rigorous evaluation of control performance and cybersecurity mechanisms. This paper presents the design, implementation, and evaluation of a real-time hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) water SCADA testbed for cyber-physical research. The proposed testbed integrates industrial control hardware, a human-machine interface, and a high-fidelity virtual water process to emulate realistic water-treatment operations. A synchronized Python-based data-logging framework collects control, process, and network measurements in real time to support quantitative analysis and dataset generation. Experimental results demonstrate stable closed-loop water-level control with a steady-state error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.8\ \text{cm}, 3.4 \%$</tex> overshoot, and an average Modbus TCP latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50\ \text{ms} (\pm 10\ \text{ms})$</tex>. The testbed provides a portable and cost-effective platform for studying water SCADA behavior and establishing baselines for future cybersecurity experimentation.

title: Evaluating Explainable AI Fidelity in Lightweight Vision Transformers Using Transfer Learning
authors: Drew Phelps, Syed Rafay Hasan, Amani Altarawneh
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: Explainable artificial intelligence is increasingly used to support trust and analysis in vision models deployed on resource constrained devices. Lightweight Vision Transformers differ substantially in architecture, capacity, and tokenization, which can influence both predictive performance and the quality of explanation guided regions. This paper presents a controlled methodology for evaluating explanation effectiveness in edge oriented Vision Transformer architectures using two retraining-based frameworks: fine-tuned fidelity and geometric remove and retrain. By pairing sufficiency oriented and necessity oriented evaluation assumptions under a fixed masking budget, the methodology enables consistent, architecture independent comparison of how explanation guided regions affect model performance under constrained inputs.

title: RGPKD: Reconstruction-Guided and Prompt-Enhanced Asymmetric Knowledge Distillation for Anomaly Detection
authors: Kaiyue Wang, Chengyan Qin, Jieru Chi, Chenglizhao Chen, Teng Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Current anomaly detection methods based on knowledge distillation typically adopt symmetric teacher–student network architectures and perform anomaly detection by localizing feature discrepancies between the two networks. However, the symmetric structure leads to insignificant feature differences in subtle anomalous regions, which tends to cause missed detection in anomalous areas. In addition, due to upsampling operations during the decoding process, the student network is prone to losing detailed information, further compromising the quality of feature reconstruction. Therefore, this article attempts to integrate a novel prompt module into the classical knowledge distillation framework and redesigns the architecture to dynamically inject rich detail information into the decoding process of the student network. Compared with existing methods, the proposed approach significantly enhances the fidelity of normal feature recovery and the salience of anomalous feature discrepancies through explicit reconstruction constraints and a detail enhancement mechanism. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves significant improvements over existing state-of-the-art techniques.

title: Exploring Consistency for Data Clustering by Multi-View Multi-Order Graph Decomposition
authors: Pengxin Xu, Chang Wu, Zhaohu Liu, Yong Peng, Feiping Nie
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Graph-based multi-view clustering aims to leverage the consistency and complementarity of multiple information sources (views) to enhance clustering performance. The introduction of multi-order graphs has brought significant performance gains by mitigating the sparsity of first-order graphs. However, different views, along with their derived high-order graphs, inevitably contain noise and view-specific information (i.e., diversity), which may hinder the learning of a consensus graph. To address this critical issue while retaining the benefits of multi-order structures, this paper proposes a novel framework, termed Consistency driven Decomposition for Multi-view Multi-order Graph Clustering (CDMMGC). In CDMMGC, multi-order graphs are utilized to mitigate the sparsity problem of first-order graphs and each multi-order graph from each view is decomposed into a consistency and a diversity component. Accordingly, the consensus graph is learned on the consistency component from multi-view multi-order graphs, which is expected to be more accurate in capturing the data semantics. Experiments on extensive datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed CDMMGC model in data clustering compared with the state-of-the-art methods. The code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/SunseaIU/CDMMGC</uri>

title: IEEE Industrial Electronics Society Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics

title: A Simple Model-Predictive Control Strategy for Discrete-Time Port-Hamiltonian Boundary Control Systems
authors: Alessandro Macchelli
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: This paper deals with discrete-time linear boundary control systems (BCSs) in port-Hamiltonian form, for which the discretization is performed in time while preserving the distributed nature of the state and the passivity of the original system. The contribution is twofold. First, an explicit parametrized expression of the discrete-time state evolution is derived. Second, this parametrization is employed to design a simple model predictive control (MPC) law. The receding-horizon scheme relies on an optimization procedure based on a quadratically constrained quadratic programming (QCQP) problem. By following standard arguments and exploiting the passivity of the BCS, we prove that the MPC methodology guarantees asymptotic stability.

title: Guidance of a human driver by an automated vehicle: Nonlinear control design via delayed spectral submanifold
authors: Bence Szaksz, Nathan van De Wouw, Gábor Stépán, Gábor Orosz
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: This paper investigates a specific human-machine interaction in which an automated vehicle (AV) provides guided control to a following human-driven vehicle (HV). We take into account both the state delay induced by human reaction time and the input delay introduced by the AV controller. The resulting dynamic model is a nonlinear delay differential equation (DDE) with two distinct constant time delays. Our objective is to design a controller for this infinite-dimensional system at the nonlinear level. First, a model reduction is performed using the delayed spectral submanifold approach. This allows us to project the dynamics onto a low-dimensional invariant manifold while capturing the essential nonlinear behavior. Based on this reduced-order model, we then design a nonlinear controller that achieves faster convergence compared to a purely linear controller. The benefits of the proposed approach for closed-loop performance are demonstrated through numerical simulations.

title: A ROS 2-Based Visual SLAM Navigation Framework: Integrating cuVSLAM and Nvblox with Nav2 Stack for Autonomous Navigation on Mobile Robot
authors: Pei Sen Wong, Mohd Ridzuan Ahmad
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: In recent years, the deployment of autonomous mobile robots has become increasingly widespread, driving a demand for robust navigation solutions in complex and unstructured environments. However, traditional systems often rely on LiDAR-based SLAM, which can be limited by the lack of semantic visual context. While Visual SLAM (VSLAM) offers a data-rich alternative by preserving visual and textural information, it also introduces substantial computational demand on embedded platforms. To address these challenges, this paper presents a ROS 2-based indoor navigation framework that integrates cuVSLAM for stereo visual-inertial localization, Nvblox for 3D reconstruction and occupancy-map generation, and the ROS 2 Navigation Stack (Nav2) for path planning and control. By combining these modules into a unified software stack, the proposed framework provides a practical implementation route for visual SLAM-based indoor navigation on embedded robotic hardware. Simulation results demonstrated low localization error for cuVSLAM, together with effective mapping and planning subsystem performance: cuVSLAM achieved high-accuracy localization with an average Relative Pose Error (RPE) of 0.0019 m and an Absolute Pose Error (APE) of 0.0814 m over trajectories up to 85.85 m. Nvblox successfully generated a 2D occupancy grid for a 53.98 m × 31.74 m environment, enabling Nav2 to execute collision-free trajectories across trials with path lengths of 25.65 m, 35.57 m, and 43.38 m. The framework was then deployed on a custom-built omnidirectional robot powered by an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, where real-world experiments demonstrated practical operation for map creation, map-based localization, and static-obstacle navigation in an indoor laboratory.

title: Model-Free Predictive Controllers for Voltage Source Inverter: A Unified Experimental Benchmarking
authors: Filippo Gemma, Jean-Baptiste Jacquier, Giulia Tresca, Jose Rodriguez, Pericle Zanchetta
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Industry Applications
abstract: This paper presents an extensive experimental comparison of different predictive current control strategies applied to a two-level voltage source inverter supplying a resistive - inductive load. All controllers were implemented on the same hardware platform and evaluated under identical operating conditions at a controller sampling frequency of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text{20}~\text{kHz}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The evaluated methods include a conventional model-based (MB) predictive controller, an auto-regressive model with exogenous input and recursive least squares estimation (ARX-RLS), and three first-order ultra-local (UL) formulations: the linear extended state observer (LESO), the least absolute shrinkage and selection operator-based estimator (LASSO) and the extended-affine (EA). Experimental results confirm that the MB achieved the lowest computational time, whereas the ARX-RLS controller attained the lowest total harmonic distortion (THD). The EA-UL method provided the best overall trade-off between harmonic performance, transient dynamics and computational effort. The study highlights that model-free-predictive controllers (MF-PC), particularly ARX-RLS and EA-UL, can deliver performance comparable to or better than MB while significantly reducing dependence on system modeling. These findings confirm the potential of MF-PC approaches as efficient and robust solutions for next-generation power electronic converters.

title: IEEE Foundation
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Control Systems

title: AI-Enhanced Spatial Cellular Traffic Demand Prediction with Contextual Clustering and Error Correction for 5G/6G Planning
authors: Mohamad Alkadamani, Colin Brown, Halim Yanikomeroglu
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Reliable cellular traffic-demand prediction supports capacity provisioning in cellular networks. Machine learning enables geospatial demand analysis by modeling nonlinear relationships between traffic demand and heterogeneous urban conditions. However, because cellular traffic demand is spatially correlated, random or location-based train/test splits can produce optimistic validation results. This letter proposes a leakage-aware methodology that combines two-stage spatial and contextual splitting with residual spatial error correction to improve spatial generalization. Across five major Canadian cities, the proposed procedure reduces mean absolute error (MAE) relative to location-only clustering. The capacity-planning impact of prediction errors is assessed through 5G NR mid-band bandwidth-dimensioning metrics.

title: Rapid Switchable Backdoor Attack on Any CLIP Model with Multiple Target Classes
authors: Bing Wang, Kun Cao, Dizhan Xue, Shengsheng Qian, Changsheng Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training) has demonstrated outstanding capability in aligning visual and textual representations, which in turn has supported numerous follow-up models and downstream applications. However, their widespread deployment also exposes them to critical security vulnerabilities, particularly under backdoor attacks. Existing backdoor attack methods typically bind a single target class through one-time training or fine-tuning, which significantly limits their threat potential and practicality. To address this limitation, we propose a novel multi-target backdoor attack framework, the Multiple Target Classes by Rapid Transformation Network (MRTNet), which enables simultaneous attacks on multiple target classes in any CLIP model and facilitates rapid switching between them, all without requiring retraining or fine-tuning of the victim model. First, we propose a novel stealthy multi-target trigger generation, which can stealthily activate multi-target attacks without being perceptible to human eyes. Second, we propose a multi-target rapid transformation network, which introduces a decoupled trigger recognizer external to the encoder and a multiple target feature rebinding mechanism, enabling efficient switching between multiple target classes without retraining the base model. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves both high stealthiness and strong attack performance, with success rates exceeding 97. 7% in different settings. We hope that this research provides new insights into the security vulnerabilities of CLIP and its downstream applications.

title: Better Hardware Could Turn Zeros Into AI Heroes: Sparse Computing Enables Leaner, Faster AI
authors: Olivia Hsu, Kalhan Koul
publish: IEEE Spectrum
abstract: Even though some artificial-intelligence experts warn that scaling up large language models (LLMs) is hitting diminishing performance returns, companies are still coming out with ever larger AI tools. Meta's latest Llama release had a staggering 2 trillion parameters that define the model.

title: Research on Caching and Power Control Optimization Strategies Based on Vehicular Networking Communication
authors: Weiguang Wang, Haixia Yang, Gaoyuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Systems Journal
abstract: The distributed multipoint and multiple transmissions in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) can lead to a significant amount of redundant transmissions, which further results in the waste of wireless channel resources and computational resources. This article aims to reduce redundant transmissions and power consumption by optimizing caching strategies and transmission power, thereby enhancing the overall energy efficiency (EE) of IoV. First, based on the stochastic geometry theory and the Zipf distribution, we derive closed-form expressions for cache hit ratio and EE. Second, we formulate an EE maximization problem for IoV that involves both caching strategies and transmission power. Given the coupled nature of these two optimization variables, the resulting EE maximization problem is NP-hard. Therefore, we propose a decoupling optimization algorithm to solve this optimization problem. Finally, we can achieve a high-quality local optimal EE of the IoV by jointly optimizing the caching strategies and transmission power. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves 51.51% and 8.35% higher EE compared to the baseline scheme and two-stage optimization scheme, respectively.

title: A Spatiotemporal Information Extraction-Based FDI Attack Detection and Data Recovery Architecture for Wind Power Prediction Systems
authors: Xuguo Jiao, Xinxin Zhao, Ruilong Deng, Shiyi Zhao, Zhenyong Zhang, Ke Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: With the increasing penetration of wind energy in power systems, forecasting wind power has emerged as an effective method to enhance system stability. However, the accuracy of wind power prediction is significantly influenced by the quality of data collected by the wind farm’s communication system, which is vulnerable to cyberattacks. The inherent strong volatility of wind energy provides natural cover for false data injection (FDI) attacks, making their detection in wind power prediction systems a very challenging problem. This paper brings new insights into FDI attack detection and data recovery through spatiotemporal information extraction for wind power prediction systems. First, wind power time series data from multiple wind turbines are decomposed into trend, seasonal, and residual terms to provide additional spatiotemporal information for feature extraction. Subsequently, a novel extended long short-term memory autoencoder framework, incorporating k-fold cross-validation and z-score-based dual-threshold strategies, is developed to reconstruct these decomposition terms for accurately and iteratively identifying FDI attacks. Further, the compromised data is recovered using a spatiotemporal graph neural network with a dynamic trust weight mechanism. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve more accurate performance than existing methods.

title: Mastering Beam Alignment for Satellite WPT: A Predictive ISAC Framework for Sustainable 6G Connectivity
authors: Min Jia, Shiyao Meng, Jinyue Song, Gaole Fan, Yihua Liu, Qing Guo
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: To tackle the distinctive challenges of satellite mobility—challenges that exert significant impacts on both the reliability of communication links and the efficiency of wireless power transfer (WPT)—we propose a distributed satellite-fusion cell-free (SFcf) integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) architecture. Traditional adaptive beamforming, which performs reactive compensation based on estimated channel state information (CSI), faces inherent limitations in achieving robust data transmission and efficient energy delivery within such high-dynamic environments, primarily due to its intrinsic latency. To surmount this bottleneck, we put forward a novel closed-loop ISAC framework that facilitates a proactive paradigm for the joint transfer of wireless information and power. The core concept lies in estimating the physical source of channel variation—namely the satellite’s motion parameters—and utilizing these parameters to enable predictive adaptation of both communication and energy beams. In this framework, sensing-derived Doppler and time of arrival (ToA) parameters are fed back to actively pre-compensate for channel mismatch, ensuring communication signals are robust and that energy beams remain precisely focused on the moving target. Therefore, our proposed SFcf-ISAC framework is specifically designed to validate this effective synergy, enabling sustainable 6G satellite-ground connectivity through high-efficiency WPT, remote sensing and resilient high-mobility communication.

title: Comprehensive Investigation of Magnetic Properties of Fe–Si and Fe-Co Alloy Under Alternating and Rotational Magnetizations
authors: Jiapeng Zhou, Shuaichao Yue, Yongjian Li, Jialin Li, Gaoxiangyu Zhou, Anouar Belahcen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: Although silicon steel remains the predominant core material in electrical machines, the increasing demand for higher torque density and compactness has motivated the investigation of Fe–Co alloys with higher saturation magnetic flux density. This paper presents a comparative analysis of the magnetic properties of Fe-Co alloy 1J22 and Fe–Si alloy B35A210 under both one-dimensional (1-D) alternating and two-dimensional (2-D) rotational magnetization, based on a self-developed 2-D magnetic measurement system. The results show that 1J22 exhibits a higher magnetic flux density level than B35A210 within the tested range. At low flux densities, the rotational loss of both materials is approximately twice the corresponding 1-D alternating loss, while this relationship no longer holds in the high-flux-density region. Loss separation results indicate that excess loss is generally larger than classical eddy-current loss before the high-flux-density knee region, whereas the eddy-current component becomes more prominent at higher flux densities. These findings provide experimental reference for magnetic material selection and loss modeling in high-power-density electrical machines.

title: Large Language Model–Driven Feature Weighting for Cross-Device Harmonization of Wearable Data: A Generalizable Framework for Digital Health Models
authors: Taeyeong Lee, Soonho Ha, Ju-Wan Kim, Min Jhon, Heon-Jeong Lee, Hwamin Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Wearable-based lifelog data offer a promising approach for digital mental health monitoring, but cohort heterogeneity, including device-specific measurement differences and variability in ecological momentary assessment (EMA) protocols, often limits model generalizability. To address this issue, we propose a large language model (LLM)-driven feature-weighting framework for cross-device harmonization in mood prediction. Leveraging few-shot learning, the framework infers cohort-specific rules from a small number of representative samples and converts them into executable weighting functions without requiring large-scale retraining. Four cohorts with distinct characteristics were used to generate cohort-specific weights, and an independent external cohort was used for validation. Model performance was evaluated by comparing classifiers before and after applying the generated weights, and traditional machine learning-based weighting methods were included as baselines to assess the validity of the LLM-based approach. Performance was measured using the area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) and F1-score across five random seeds. LLM-based weighting improved external validation F1-score more consistently than machine learning-based weighting, particularly when GPT-OSS was used. SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) and correlation analyses indicated that the generated weights were actively used by the predictive models and were associated with relevant physiological and behavioral features. These findings suggest that LLM-driven rule-based feature weighting may improve the external generalizability of wearable-based digital health models across heterogeneous cohorts.

title: A Novel Bidirectional Multiport Converter for Integration of Renewable Energy and Storage Systems
authors: Hesam Soroushzad, José Manuel Damil Vicente, Adriano Da Silva Carvalho, Agostinho Afonso Da Rocha
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The integration of heterogeneous energy sources in modern power systems requires multiport converters that balance structural simplicity with high operational flexibility. This paper proposes a novel bidirectional multiport DC-AC topology, termed X-Bridge, which employs a symmetric four-switch architecture to manage multiple independent power paths. Unlike conventional H-bridge configurations, the X-Bridge facilitates the seamless integration of heterogeneous sources without increasing the semiconductor count. Furthermore, a modified SPWM strategy is implemented that limits the switching and conduction intervals of each semiconductor to only 50% of the fundamental period. In contrast, conventional unipolar and bipolar methods require continuous device activity throughout the entire cycle. The performance of the proposed converter is validated through both PLECS simulations and a hardware prototype. Results confirm a robust dynamic response, maintaining a stable power output even under simultaneous source-voltage mismatch and abrupt load transients. The X-Bridge offers a reliable, fault-tolerant, and cost-effective solution for hybrid renewable energy and modular applications.

title: Decentralized End-to-End Multi-AAV Pursuit Using Predictive Spatio-Temporal Observation Via Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yude Li, Zhexuan Zhou, Huizhe Li, Yanke Sun, Yenan Wu, Yichen Lai, Yiming Wang, Youmin Gong, Jie Mei
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Decentralized cooperative pursuit in cluttered environments is challenging for autonomous aerial swarms, especially under partial and noisy perception. Existing methods often rely on abstracted geometric features or privileged ground-truth states, and therefore sidestep perceptual uncertainty in real-world settings. We propose a decentralized end-to-end multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) framework that maps raw LiDAR observations directly to continuous control commands. Central to the framework is the Predictive Spatio-Temporal Observation (PSTO), an egocentric grid representation that aligns obstacle geometry with predictive adversarial intent and teammate motion in a unified, fixed-resolution projection. Built on PSTO, a single decentralized policy enables agents to navigate static obstacles, intercept dynamic targets, and maintain cooperative encirclement. Simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior capture efficiency and competitive success rates compared to state-of-the-art learning-based approaches relying on privileged obstacle information. Furthermore, the unified policy scales seamlessly across different team sizes without retraining. Finally, fully autonomous outdoor experiments validate the framework on a quadrotor swarm relying on only onboard sensing and computing. Project details are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://hitsz-mas.github.io/psto-aav-pursuit/</uri>.

title: The Path I Didn’t Plan, but Chose: Finding My Place in Engineering [Women in Engineering]
authors: Verena Marterer
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine

title: Overpass Ledger: Full Parallelization and Fast Re-Execution for High-Performance
authors: Mingrui Cao, Bin Cao, Weihao Peng, Mugen Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: Permissioned blockchain systems provide a mutual-trust platform for data sharing and collaboration among organizations. However, performance bottlenecks limit their adoption in industrial applications requiring high transaction throughput and low latency. Recent advancements have focused on leveraging parallelism to improve performance, but transaction contention remains a significant challenge. The Optimistic Concurrency Control (OCC) mechanism, once widely used to manage transaction contention in permissioned blockchains, is valued for its simplicity and minimal design constraints. However, its reliance on the strategy of aborting conflicting transactions results in resource wastage and suboptimal performance, rendering it less favorable in recent research. This paper presents the Overpass Ledger (OPL), a high-performance permissioned blockchain system that utilizes an overpass-inspired workflow. To address transaction contention in such a highly parallelized workflow, the OCC mechanism is revisited and Re-Execution (ReX) is proposed, an enhanced OCC variant that efficiently re-executes conflicting transactions to eliminate transaction abortion and maximize resource utilization. By integrating ReX, OPL fully harnesses the advantages of parallel stage processing and concurrent transaction execution. Experimental results demonstrate that OPL achieves throughput improvements of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$77\times$</tex-math></inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$19\times$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> compared to Hyperledger Fabric, BIDL, and FISCO BCOS, respectively, while maintaining consistently low latency.

title: Advanced Electromagnetics Methods for Practical Engineering Applications [Guest Editorial]
authors: Constantine Sideris
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine

title: FedDD: Combating Federated Noisy Label Learning with Intra- and Inter-Distributional Difference
authors: Tong Jin, Chen-Chen Zong, Sheng-Jun Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables multiple parties to collaboratively train a global machine learning model without sharing local data. While FL has made awe-inspiring advances, most existing studies assume fully clean data labels on clients, which rarely holds in real-world scenarios where label noise is prevalent and detrimental to FL performance. Prior approaches typically rely on the small-loss criterion to identify noisy instances, which has been proven to struggle in highly heterogeneous settings. To overcome this, we propose a robust framework called FedDD that tackles label noise in FL by leveraging intra-and inter-distributional differences. Instead, FedDD employs an intra-difference measure, which utilizes the intra-logit variance to preliminarily identify the clean subset. Subsequently, it refines the data by introducing an inter-distributional difference metric based on prediction discrepancies between local and global models. To further utilize identified noisy examples, a novel federated semi-supervised learning paradigm is proposed that amalgamates knowledge from the global and local models to guide pseudo-labeling. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets under both independent and identically distributed (IID) and non-IID settings demonstrate the superiority of FedDD over state-of-the-art methods.

title: HCDi-Fusion: Hybrid-Conditioned Diffusion Model for Cross-Resolution Multimodal Image Fusion
authors: Duzhong Feng, Chenxi Qiu, Tao Yue, Xuemei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In multimodal image fusion tasks, introducing diffusion models faces a critical challenge due to the lack of ground truth (GT) for step-wise supervision during the diffusion process. Existing methods typically unfold diffusion models in two ways: either supervising only the final fused result while neglecting intermediate diffusion steps, or adopting mask-based fusion strategies that rely on pre-generated masks to supervise intermediate predicted masks. However, the latter heavily depends on the quality of the pre-generated masks. Moreover, in real-world scenarios, the spatial resolutions of different modalities often exhibit significant discrepancies, which further complicates the generation of reliable pre-generated masks. To address these challenges, we propose HCDi-Fusion for cross-resolution multimodal image fusion. Specifically, the Hybrid-Conditioned Diffusion (HCD) model performs guided denoising by jointly leveraging self-conditioning and external conditioning, which considers inter-step relationships and different resolution discrepancies. Furthermore, the proposed Self-conditioned Step-Wise Fusion (SSWF) model enables progressive fusion in the latent feature space across diffusion steps. To accommodate the inherently unfolded nature of diffusion models in fusion tasks, we further design a Step-Wise Supervision Mechanism to supervise intermediate diffusion steps. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple fusion tasks, diverse resolution discrepancies, multiple datasets, and a wide range of evaluation metrics.

title: A Lightweight Chaotic Map-Based Authentication Scheme for Secure Wearable Computing
authors: Weizheng Wang, Qipeng Xie, Guanjie Cheng, Kaixin Chen, Zhiquan Liu, Kaishun Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Wearable computing enables continuous sensing and cloud analytics, but sensitive physiological data remain vulnerable to eavesdropping, tampering, hijacking, and traceability across wireless and cloud links. Because wearable devices have tight battery, computation, memory, and bandwidth limits, heavyweight authentication is impractical. Existing schemes either rely on costly operations, incur multiple communication rounds, or remain vulnerable to de-synchronization and ephemeral-secret-leakage attacks in multi-party settings. To address these issues, we present a lightweight chaotic map-based authentication and key-exchange scheme for cloud-assisted wearable computing. The scheme combines Chebyshev polynomials, hash functions, and lightweight symmetric protection to realize mutual authentication among wearable devices, mobile terminals, and cloud servers while establishing secure session keys and supporting dynamic pseudonym renewal. Informal analysis shows resistance to replay, de-synchronization, man-in-the-middle, password-guessing, traceability, and ephemeral-secret-leakage attacks while preserving anonymity and forward secrecy. Formal analysis in the Real-Or-Random model proves session-key indistinguishability against classical polynomial-time adversaries under the CMDLP/CMCDHP assumptions. Compared with a recent ASCON-based three-party baseline, the scheme reduces total computation and communication costs by 28.8% and 28.7%, respectively. These results show a practical security-efficiency balance for resource-constrained healthcare wearables.

title: SCRAP LINK: Smart Scrap Collection and Trading System : Revolutionizing Scrap Management: A Web-Based Platform for Efficient Scrap Management
authors: Vaishnavi U S, Prathik K, Mrudula U, Deepak P N, Aishwarya M Bhat
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: The increasing difficulty in managing waste and recycling shows the need for smart digital platforms that make the recycling process easier. SCRAP LINK is a web-based system that connects scrap sellers directly with approved recyclers, limiting intermediaries to ensure fair and transparent pricing. The platform uses Machine Learning, specifically a Random Forest Regression model, to predict current scrap prices based on past market data. Sellers can list their materials by providing details such as type, quantity, and location, while recyclers can search and filter these listings based on location and material needs. The system was built using React.js, Typescript.js, and PostgreSQL, which helps it scale well, handle data efficiently, and run smoothly. Through geolocation features using Leaflet, recyclers can find nearby scrap sources, helping cut down on transportation costs and time. In general, SCRAP LINK brings digital tools to the scrap trading world, supporting transparency, sustainability, and the principles of a circular economy through smart automation.

title: Melanoma Classification using Adaptive Nutcracker Optimization -based Dense Residual Dual-Shuffle Attention Network using skin images
authors: Vijaya Padmanabha, Mohamed Sirajudeen Yoosuf, Joseph Mani, Roshan Fernandes, Anisha P Rodrigues, Manjunath Kamath K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Melanoma is formed by the uncontrollable growing of melanocytes. The occurrence of melanoma has increased due to factors, like radiation exposure, excessive Ultraviolet (UV), and genetic susceptibility. Moreover, it poses a severe threat to human health and causes a high mortality rate. The timely classification of melanoma is essential to provide effective treatment and improve the survival of patients. Still, the diagnostic process suffers from inter-observer variability and demands long training times, as well as high computational power. For addressing such issues, the hybrid model named Adaptive Nutcracker Optimization-based Dense Residual Dual-Shuffle Attention Network (ANO_DRDA-Net)-based Melanoma classification is developed in this paper. Initially, the hairline detection and removal processing of skin image is carried out by the Artificial Intelligence-based Hairline Removal (AI-HR) method. The B-spline-based active contour model is employed for segmenting the lesion. Finally, the disease classification is done by DRDA-Net, and the devised ANO trains its hyperparameters. In addition, the accuracy, True Positive Rate (TPR), and True Negative Rate (TNR) metrics are used for estimating the performance of ANO_DRDA-Net, and it yields the corresponding values of 96.39%, 96.63%, and 95.44%.

title: An Ensembled Deep Learning Approach for Prognosis of Dysgraphia in Children through Handwritten Images
authors: Sailaja Mulakaluri, Sivagami S, Thejaswi Nandyala, S. Annie Christila, Amruta Gadad, Ms.Saranya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Dysgraphia is a neurological learning disorder that affects handwriting and written expression, often hindering a child’s academic progress if not identified early. This paper proposes an ensembled deep learning model combining a Super-Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (SRGAN) and Residual Neural Network (ResNet) for the prognosis of Dysgraphia in children using handwritten images. The SRGAN enhances handwriting samples by generating high-resolution images, enabling better feature extraction, while ResNet ensures effective learning through deep feature representation. The ensemble approach integrates predictions from multiple models to enhance accuracy and reduce generalization errors. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system achieves superior performance in terms of accuracy, precision, and recall compared to traditional models. This study offers a robust and automated framework for early Dysgraphia detection, supporting timely educational interventions and personalized learning strategies.

title: Text-to-SQL Agent: An Iterative Question Rewriting Framework Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Sixuan Wang, Yongjun Zhong, Dongjin Yu, Donghai Li, Chenming Wu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Agents
abstract: None

title: Polarization- and Mutual-Coupling-Aware Rotatable Antennas Enabled Wireless Communications: Modeling, Orientation Optimization, and Prototyping
authors: Jianchuan Wei, Simin Song, Zhenyu Kang, Tengjiao Wang, Huaqiang Gao, Shanpu Shen, Xiaoming Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Rotatable antennas (RAs) expand the spatial degrees of freedom of multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems beyond conventional fixed-panel arrays. However, existing evaluations often model rotation merely as a scalar gain adjustment and overlook three hardware-critical effects: 1) the phase carried by the antenna pattern, 2) rotation-induced changes in the polarization, and 3) orientation-dependent mutual coupling variations. In this context, we develop a unified modeling-evaluation-optimization framework that explicitly incorporates phase, polarization, and mutual coupling effects through a rotation-explicit complex vector element pattern (CVEP). Polarization coupling and mismatch are captured by embedding the rotated CVEPs into a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 2$ </tex-math></inline-formula> per-ray polarization structure, while mutual coupling variation is modeled via power-consistent interpolation of embedded radiation patterns under joint orientation states. Additionally, a compact RA base-station (RA-BS) element and a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$6\times 3$ </tex-math></inline-formula> RA-BS prototype supporting ±60° mechanical rotation with a high front-to-back ratio are designed, assembled, and measured. The simulation and measurement results validate that the proposed electromagnetic (EM)-consistent RA system model accurately characterizes rotation-induced variations in gain, polarization, and radiation patterns. Furthermore, an alternating feasible-direction optimization algorithm, tailored to the jointly programmable BS- and subarray-level rotations, is developed to maximize the downlink sum-rate in multi-user MIMO deployments. Our simulations demonstrate that the proposed rotation-aware RA-BS architecture significantly outperforms conventional gain-only and mutual-coupling-neglected baselines. These findings highlight the necessity of incorporating phase, polarization, and mutual coupling effects into RA modeling and optimization to ensure robust performance under uniform user distributions and maximize achievable throughput in hotspot deployments.

title: Smart Platform for Effortless Assignment Submission and Precision Grading
authors: Pratheek H, Mohammed Muzammil, Panishthi Shetty, Rida Khadeeja, Vaishnavi Shetty
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: GradeMate is a smart, web-based platform designed to simplify and enhance the assignment submission and grading workflow for both students and educators. The system addresses common academic challenges such as delayed submissions, inconsistent grading, limited feedback, and high faculty workload. By integrating Django-based backend services with Hugging Face transformer models, GradeMate automates the evaluation of student assignments and generates clear, meaningful feedback that supports continuous learning.The platform provides dedicated dashboards for students and instructors, enabling secure assignment uploads, deadline tracking, real-time notifications, and transparent grade management. The AI Evaluation Engine processes submissions, predicts grades, and generates feedback, while a rule-based validation layer ensures fairness, accuracy, and alignment with academic rubrics. All evaluation records are securely stored, supporting accountability and easy auditability.Designed with accessibility, user-friendliness, and scalability in mind, GradeMate offers a dependable digital solution suitable for modern classrooms and learning environments. By combining automation with human oversight, the system reduces manual effort, enhances transparency, and promotes a more effective and engaging learning experience.

title: Real-Time Phishing Threat Detection Using Hybrid Deep Learning and Multi-Domain URL–Email Analysis
authors: Akhilraj V. Gadagkar, Shifana Begum, Rameesa K
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Phishing continues to pose a significant threat across multiple digital communication platforms, including email, instant messaging, pop-up prompts, and deceptive web pages. This work presents a predictive system designed to identify malicious URLs using datasets obtained from PhishTank and the University of New Brunswick, which together provide diverse collections of phishing, benign, spam, malware, and defacement URLs. A combination of traditional machine learning classifiers and deep learning architectures was explored to achieve reliable detection performance. The proposed phishing detector framework incorporates CNN, LSTM, and hybrid CNN–LSTM models to examine email headers, message content, embedded links, and attached resources for indicators of phishing activity. The system operates in real time, monitoring inbound and outbound email traffic and blocking suspicious attempts without interrupting existing workflows. The expected outcomes include improved detection accuracy, fewer false positives, and strengthened organizational defenses. By integrating advanced classification methods with continuous threat analysis, the proposed approach offers a scalable and effective mechanism for protecting both individuals and enterprises from evolving phishing attacks.

title: VPN Queue Performance Analysis Using Matrix Analytics Methods
authors: Idris Yusuf, Wei Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: The post-COVID era has witnessed a significant increase in remote work policies, leading to the rapid adoption of Virtual Private Network (VPN) technology. Although VPNs are not new, their widespread deployment has raised important concerns regarding performance evaluation. In a typical VPN connection, users are first authenticated during Phase 1 before being allowed to transmit and receive data through a secure tunnel in Phase 2, which relies on the successful completion of the authentication phase. By modeling the VPN system as a two-phase service process within a single-server queueing framework, this study establishes a theoretical foundation for VPN performance analysis using the Matrix Analytic Method (MAM). Our analysis demonstrates that the VPN system can be characterized as a Level-Dependent Quasi-Birth-Death (LDQBD) process. The corresponding infinitesimal generator matrix exhibits a block-tridiagonal structure, in which transitions occur only between neighboring states and the block size increases with each level. Using MAM, we analyze the steady-state behavior of the queueing system and derive the initial state probability vector. Based on this formulation, several key VPN performance metrics are obtained. Numerical analyses are conducted to validate the theoretical results and confirm the accuracy of the proposed analytical model.

title: A Single-Layer Pseudo-Quantum Creep-In Mechanism for Time-Series Forecasting
authors: V Lakshmi Chetana, T Sai Nithin, Ch Subhash Nandan, D Amarnath Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: Time-series prediction is important to numerous applications, such as energy systems, climate analytics, and industrial maintenance. Classical sequential neural networks, e.g. RNN, GRU and LSTM, have shown good performance in modeling long-term temporal dependencies, but the expressiveness of these models are limited by their non-linear feature representation ability. We present a hybrid forecasting method that incorporates a pseudo-quantum layer with a classical LSTM architecture, thus allowing sin-based phased modulation to emulate interference-based transformations of features. We conduct the performance evaluation of the proposed Hybrid-L1 model on transformer temperature ETTh1 dataset and compare it with the baselines namely RNN, GRU and LSTM. Performance is evaluated using standard regression metrics (MSE and MAE). The comparative study illustrates that adding the pseudo-quantum layer enhances predictive reliability and pattern learning speed for sequential data, suggesting that quantum-inspired algebraic feature modulation can enhance sequential learning in complex temporal settings even in the absence of physical quantum hardware.

title: Intelligent Feature Selection for Semiconductor Manufacturing Using a Hybrid Genetic Algorithm and Neural Network
authors: K. Padmapriya, K. Kavitha, R. Senthamil Selvan, Basi Reddy A., Manisha Bhimrao Mane
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Secure IoT, Assured and Trusted Computing (SATC)
abstract: When it comes to manufacturing processes, “Smart Manufacturing” refers to using optimization strategies that employ sophisticated analytics methodologies. Optimal and efficient methods of data management are becoming increasingly important as the number of Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) sensors used in production processes continue to rise. Intelligent and effective automation is possible with the help of Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence applied to production data. To make semiconductor production smarter, we undertake a thorough examination in this work using Deep Learning and Evolutionary Computing techniques. We provide an algorithm that can adapt to new situations in order to overcome obstacles and gain deeper insights into semiconductor production. This work describes in more detail how an intelligent feature selection algorithm may be developed by combining a Genetic Algorithm with a Neural Network. It aims to shed light on several aspects that enable firms to access effective predictive technologies and offer a cutting-edge solution for regulating industrial processes.

title: Current-Sensorless Virtual-Vector Quasi-Sliding Mode Predictive Control for T-Type Inverters
authors: Sheng Huang, Yong Yang, Jianyu Pan, Jiefeng Hu, Xinan Zhang, Chunyang Zhang, Jose Rodriguez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: To alleviate the tedious weighting factor tuning, heavy computational burden, and inherent chattering in traditional model predictive control (MPC) and sliding mode control (SMC) for T-type inverters, this paper proposes a current-sensorless virtual-vector quasi-sliding-mode predictive control (VVQSMPC) strategy. By embedding discrete sliding-mode dynamics into the predictive control cost function, a unified control framework is established that integrates the fast dynamic response of MPC and the strong robustness of SMC while effectively suppressing chattering. To achieve neutral-point voltage balance, a set of virtual voltage vectors is constructed as convex combinations of basic vectors, thereby eliminating weighting factors and preserving the intrinsic relationship between the modulation index and output voltage amplitude. A three-stage vector preselection strategy is further developed to reduce the number of evaluated vectors from 69 to 14 or 16, significantly lowering computational complexity. In addition, a full-order state observer is employed to enable current-sensorless operation and enhance system reliability. Discrete-time Lyapunov analysis demonstrates that the proposed method ensures uniformly bounded quasi-sliding-mode motion within a limited bandwidth. Experimental results on a DSP-based platform demonstrate that, compared with conventional MPC, the proposed strategy achieves improved steady-state voltage quality, faster transient response, and enhanced robustness against parameter variations. These results confirm that the proposed VVQSMPC provides a practical and effective solution for high-performance control of T-type inverters.

title: Cardinal-Ordinal Consistency Adjustment Driven Group Consensus Decision Making With Distributed Preference Relations and Application in Remanufacturing Quality Management_supp1-3694094.docx
authors: Mi Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: The consistency of expert’s assessment and consensus reaching process (CRP) is a hot topic in group decision making (GDM). When an expert provides the comparison between different alternative pairs, how to measure the consistency degree and adjust inconsistent judgments is important. In this article, the construction of complete cardinal consistent distributed preference relations (DPRs) from multiple routes is first proposed. The advantage lies in that all possible results could be captured instead of just depending on adjacent alternative pairs. Then, the ordinal consistency measurement of DPRs is provided based on the ranking order dissimilarity derived from possibility degree. Second, the concepts of soft and strict consistent DPRs are given, which deduce four consistency types, i.e., perfect consistent, partial consistent, partial inconsistent, and completely inconsistent. We also discuss the modeling of consistent DPR matrix when adding new alternatives. Third, several optimization models are established to adjust inconsistent DPRs under different consistency types. These models all minimize the modification extent of individual’s original judgment. Ordinal consensus index is then defined, followed by the optimization model to make experts reach ordinal consensus while maintaining cardinal and ordinal consistency. A case study about the selection of a dismantling scheme in remanufacturing quality management process is finally provided to illustrate the effectiveness and validity of the proposed method.

title: Adaptive Dynamic Functional Connectivity with Deep Spatio-Temporal Fusion for High-Accuracy Identification of Major Depressive Disorder
authors: Hongqiang Qiao, Jing Jie, Ming Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: The pathogenesis of major depressive disorder (MDD) has not been fully elucidated, and early identification and intervention are the most effective approach. Dynamic functional connectivity (dFC) is an effective method for revealing functional abnormalities in brain diseases. However, mainstream dFC based on sliding window methods suffers from parameter-sensitive limitations and lacks stability and reproducibility. To address this, we propose an adaptive dFC estimation framework that combines a deep spatio-temporal feature fusion model to identify MDD. Firstly, the instantaneous phase is calculated to construct an ultra-functional magnetic resonance imaging time series to enhance the representation of rapid neural dynamics. Secondly, the Ultra-weighted sparse partial correlation (UWSPC) is applied to detect functional connectivity, and the exponentially weighted dynamic covariance algorithm is further applied to achieve adaptive dFC estimation. Finally, the spatio-temporal features are extracted from the dFC time series and fused through a deep neural network to capture higher-order discriminative patterns. After we applied our framework to the SRPBS multi-disease MRI dataset, the accuracy for MDD classification reached 91.36%, which is at least 9.9 percentage points higher than that of the current state-of-the-art methods. Interpretability analysis (SHAP and feature tracking) revealed abnormal dynamic interactions in MDD, specifically within the default mode network, anterior cingulate cortex, thalamus, and cerebellum. These biomarkers have the potential to accurately distinguish individual patients with MDD from healthy controls. This may facilitate the development of a valuable MDD diagnostic tool based on rsfMRI.

title: Self-Assembled Monolayer Functionalized Q-BIC Terahertz Metasurfaces for High-Sensitivity Biosensing_supp1-3697738.docx
authors: Pibin Bing
publish: N/A
abstract: Self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) provide a controllable interfacial engineering strategy for terahertz (THz) sensing by enabling precise tuning of electromagnetic boundary conditions. In this work, a sensing platform based on quasi-bound states (Q-BIC) in the continuum THz metasurfaces is developed, integrating SAMs-enabled surface functionalization with colloidal gold to construct a synergistically engineered interface. This hybrid configuration allows coordinated modulation of resonance characteristics and interfacial electromagnetic responses, leading to markedly improved sensing performance. The metasensor incorporating SAMs-functionalized colloidal gold exhibits the highest sensitivity, achieving a limit of detection of 19.59 pg/mL for bovine serum albumin（BSA）, with a resonance peak modulation depth 3.6 times greater than that of the colloidal gold-only metasensor. The enhancement primarily originates from the strong near-field confinement induced by the Q-BIC mode and the spatially controlled distribution of colloidal gold within hotspot regions enabled by SAMs functionalization. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively leverages synergistic interfacial engineering and nanostructure integration, providing a robust pathway for developing high-performance THz biosensors based on Q-BIC metasurfaces.

title: Strong Saddle-Shaped Geometry of Depth Maps Can Boost Multi-View Stereo
authors: Zekun Gu, Tiao Tan, Zongqing Lu, Jing-Hao Xue, Qingmin Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Multi-view stereo (MVS) aims to accurately estimate depth maps for high-quality 3D reconstruction. Recently, saddle-shaped depth maps have been proved to improve the quality of 3D reconstruction. However, existing methods often suffer from weak saddle-shaped geometry in the estimated depth maps and high redundancy in network design, leading to suboptimal reconstruction performance and low computational efficiency. In this paper, we propose SDMVSNet, a new model to show that stronger saddle-shaped depth map can achieve better MVS performance while maintaining high model efficiency. Specifically, we introduce a novel multi-granularity loss function to enforce strong and comprehensive saddle-shaped geometry of the depth map. Moreover, we propose a spatial folding regularization, which leverages the spectral bias of neural networks to produce smooth submaps for better reconstruction of saddle-shaped depth map. In addition, we propose a saddle-aware feature transformer to effectively capture long-range saddle-shaped geometric information via a lightweight attention mechanism. Extensive experiments on the DTU, BlendedMVS, Tanks and Temples and ETH3D datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art MVS performance with high efficiency.

title: HEPE++: Towards VLM-based Hybrid Explainable Prompt Enhancement for Zero-Shot Industrial Anomaly Detection
authors: Weichao Cai, Weiliang Huang, Yunkang Cao, Chao Huang, Bob Zhang, Fei Yuan, Jie Wen, Weiming Shen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Zero-Shot Industrial Anomaly Detection (ZSIAD) aims to identify and localize anomalies in industrial images from unseen categories. Owing to the powerful generalization capabilities, Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved growing interest in ZSIAD. To guide the model toward understanding and localizing the semantically complex industrial anomalies, existing VLM-based methods have attempted to provide additional prompts to the model through learnable text prompt templates. However, these zero-shot methods lack detailed descriptions of specific anomalies, making it difficult to classify and segment the diverse range of industrial anomalies accurately. To address the aforementioned issue, we propose an effective solution, which includes a multi-stage prompt generation agent and a Hybrid Explainable Prompt Enhancement plus (HEPE++) framework. Specifically, we leverage the Multi-modal Language Large Model (MLLM) to articulate the detailed differential information between normal and test samples, which can provide detailed text prompts to the model through further refinement and anti-false alarm constraint. Moreover, we introduce the Visual Fundamental Model (VFM) to generate anomaly-related attention prompts for more accurate localization of anomalies with varying sizes and shapes. Building on this foundation, HEPE++ jointly utilizes generated detailed text and attention prompts to enhance the performance of VLM-based methods in ZSIAD. Furthermore, we introduce a novel gated attention mechanism to augment the model’s ability to focus on potential subtle anomalous regions in the foreground. Extensive experiments on seven real-world industrial anomaly detection datasets have shown that the proposed method not only outperforms recent SOTA methods, but also its explainable prompts provide the model with a more intuitive basis for anomaly identification.

title: A Novel Physics-Informed Transformer Algorithm (PITA) for Near&#x2013;Real-Time Synergistic Multi-Parameter Aerosol Retrieval From FY-4B/AGRI Geostationary Satellite_supp1-3700045.docx
authors: Tianhe Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate retrieval of aerosol properties from geostationary satellites is critical for capturing rapid atmospheric variability, yet remains difficult due to uncertain aerosol-model assumptions, clear-pixel misclassification over bright surfaces, and limited physical interpretability of purely data-driven approaches. We present a Physics-Informed Transformer Algorithm (PITA) for aerosol retrieval that couples radiative transfer (RT) physics with a Transformer architecture to enable near – real-time synergistic retrieval of aerosol optical depth (AOD) at 550 nm and the Ångström exponent (AE; 440–675 nm) from Fengyun-4B (FY-4B) Advanced Geosynchronous Radiation Imager (AGRI) observations. PITA integrates: 1) six regionally optimized seasonal aerosol models derived via deep clustering of 11-year ground-based aerosol observations, reducing the mean absolute error (MAE) of forward-simulated apparent reflectance by 57.6% relative to MODIS Collection 6 models; 2) a critical-reflectance clear-pixel identification scheme to improve screening over bright surfaces; and 3) an RT-constrained Transformer network with RT-derived features, with SHAP attribution indicating these physics features contribute > 70% of model importance. Sample-based ten-fold cross-validation yields AOD performance of R = 0.94 and MAE = 0.07 (76% within the expected error, EE) and AE performance of R = 0.77 and MAE = 0.16 (93% within EE). Compared with the operational FY-4B land aerosol product, PITA improves AOD correlation by 52.5% and increases AE correlation from − 0.12 to 0.73. Sensitivity analyses show negligible bias at low aerosol loading (AOD < 0.23) and weak dependence on particle size. Without external reanalysis inputs, PITA offers a physically interpretable framework for near – real-time, high-accuracy geostationary aerosol monitoring.
