title: Safety-Certified Distributed Formation for Uncertain Multirobot Systems With Minimum Restrictive Connectivity Maintenance
authors: Xiaofeng Zhao, Yunkai Lv, Zhuping Wang, Hao Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article addresses the distributed formation control problem for safety-certified uncertain multirobot systems with minimum restrictive connectivity maintenance (MRCM). Compared to this problem with deterministic models, it presents inherently more challenging requirements for safety and stability analysis. An integrated quadratic programming controller incorporating input-to-state safety control barrier functions (ISSf-CBFs) is developed to simultaneously enforce safety certification and MRCM. This controller builds upon a nominal design leveraging input-to-state stability and ISSf lemmas. The derived ISSf-CBFs provide formal guarantees for both safety certification and MRCM under uncertainties. The key to obtaining this controller lies in constructing ISSf-CBFs that enable safety certification and MRCM, from which the corresponding constraints are formally derived. Unlike existing methods, the strategy maximizes formation performance by executing formation tasks at full capability whenever possible, and only modifying the nominal controller when safety or MRCM constraints are violated. Finally, simulation and experiment validate the effectiveness of the proposed controller.

title: HierCIM: A 16-Kb SRAM-Based Digital CIM Macro With Hierarchical Adder-Tree Accumulation for Edge CNN Inference
authors: Vikash Vishwakarma, Gopal Raut, Amit Mittal, Naman Goyal, Baker Mohammad, Santosh Kumar Vishvakarma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: The growing demand for efficient deep-learning inference on edge platforms requires hardware that is both energy-efficient and practically implementable. This work presents a 16-Kb all-digital static random-access memory (SRAM)-based compute-in-memory (CIM) macro for low-bit CNN inference, featuring a hierarchical adder-tree-based accumulation architecture. The design integrates a <sc xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">nor</small>-enabled SRAM compute cell, column-wise rearrangement network, sparsity-aware compression, and multistage hierarchical accumulation within a 64-bank <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$64 \,\, \times \,\, 4$</tex-math> </inline-formula> architecture, enabling scalable bit-serial processing and utilization-aware mapping. Implemented in 65-nm CMOS, the macro achieves 8.19 TOPS effective throughput at 1.0 V and a peak energy efficiency of 586 TOPS/W at 0.9 V under practical operating conditions. Hardware-compatible CNN mapping is demonstrated using LeNet-5, VGG-8, and ResNet-8. The design achieves 98.1% and 72.3% accuracy on MNIST and CIFAR-10, respectively, with 1-bit activations and 4-bit weights, while 4-bit configurations on deeper networks show only 3%–4% degradation from FP32 baselines. CNN inference is evaluated using a hardware-compatible post-training quantization (PTQ) flow without retraining. These results demonstrate that the proposed SRAM-CIM architecture provides an efficient and scalable accumulation solution with a practical tradeoff among throughput, energy efficiency, and implementability for edge-oriented deep neural network (DNN) inference.

title: Domain Decomposition-Based DGTD Method With Automated Implicit–Explicit Classification for Multiscale Electromagnetic Modeling
authors: Youshen Tian, Heng Cao, Zhao Guo, Lixiao Wang, Qingtao Sun, Wen Chen, Qing Huo Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this article, an efficient domain decomposition-based discontinuous Galerkin time-domain (DGTD) method with automated implicit–explicit classification is proposed to facilitate the modeling of large-scale and multiscale electromagnetic problems. By adopting an operating-wavelength-based domain decomposition strategy, the computational domain can be automatically partitioned into multiple nonoverlapping explicit and implicit subdomains for arbitrary geometries. Specifically, electrically small structures containing elements with edge lengths much smaller than the operating wavelength are isolated and solved by the implicit time integration scheme, whereas subdomains dominated by large-scale features and discretized using coarse meshes with relatively large edge lengths are treated explicitly. This automated domain decomposition, together with the corresponding implicit–explicit time integration scheme, eliminates the need for carefully prescribed subdomain partitioning and substantially enhances the flexibility of the subdomain-level DGTD method for complex electromagnetic problems. Several representative numerical examples are presented and discussed to demonstrate the computational efficiency and flexibility of the proposed method with respect to the conventional DGTD schemes.

title: Performance Analysis and Optimization of Carrier Tracking PLL under Noise in GNSS Receivers
authors: Zhiqiang Gong, Gang Ou, Hanbo Gao, Honglei Lin, Zhe Liu, Tisheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The rise of miniaturized wearable devices has imposed more stringent requirements on the sensitivity of satellite signal reception. However, the low-cost oscillators commonly used in wearable devices exhibit considerable frequency instability, which degrades both receiver sensitivity and tracking accuracy. To address this issue, this paper establishes a tracking jitter model that jointly accounts for thermal noise and oscillator noise, and derives an explicit expression for tracking jitter as a function of the coherent integration time. Based on the U-shaped characteristic of the resulting tracking jitter curve, an optimal coherent integration time model is further derived. In addition, the time-varying behaviors of tracking jitter contributed by different oscillator noise components are investigated, thereby characterizing the dominant role of flicker frequency noise. Both simulation and experimental results show that, for most integration intervals, flicker frequency noise is the primary source of oscillator-induced tracking jitter. Compared with several conventional coherent integration time settings, the proposed optimal coherent integration time model improves tracking accuracy by 3% to 49% and enhances sensitivity by 0.5 to 1 dB. In comparison with the Kalman filter-based optimal coherent integration method, the proposed approach achieves comparable sensitivity while preserving the low-complexity advantage of the conventional PLL, and further improves steady-state tracking accuracy by approximately 2% to 4%. These results provide a theoretical basis for oscillator selection and parameter configuration in satellite signal receiving terminals.

title: Deep Learning-Based Predictive ISAC for Backscatter Vehicular Networks
authors: Boyu Yang, Liyazhou Hu, Han Zhu, Edmundo Monteiro
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) enabled by backscatter communication is emerging as a promising paradigm for dynamic vehicular networks. However, the inherent decision latency in such systems poses a critical performance challenge, causing a mismatch between the executed decision and the actual channel state. To tackle this challenge, we propose a novel deep learning (DL)-based predictive framework utilizing a customized long short-term memory (LSTM) network to accurately forecast the continuous kinematic evolution of the system states from sequential historical measurements. These predictions then enable proactive transmit beamforming, which in turn effectively compensates for system latency to maximize end-to-end performance. Comprehensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed predictive method achieves near-optimal performance and exhibits significant superiority in dynamic vehicular scenarios.

title: Multi-Objective Evolutionary Policy Learning Aided Resource Management for SCMA LEO Transmission
authors: Zan Li, Qing Wei, Jia Shi, Pei Xiao, Rahim Tafazolli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: We investigate the resource management in a sparse code multiple access (SCMA) enabled downlink LEO satellite communication system with multi-beam capability. It considers the joint optimization of beamwidth, subcarrier, and SCMA codebook of the system aiming to minimize the average transmission delay while maximizing the coverage probability under dynamic user distributions. To tackle the exponentially large discrete action space, we propose a MAP-Elites enhanced Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (ME-MORL) framework that combines discrete-action Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with an evolutionary archive to maintain diverse resource allocation policies while avoiding premature convergence. Extensive experiments demonstrate that in unseen scenarios, it attains the target coverage with only 50% of the training iterations required by competing approaches while maintaining a 24% lower transmission delay, demonstrating superior sample efficiency and cross-scenario generalization.

title: Effect of Dielectric Barrier Discharge Plasma-Activated Water on Growth Characteristics of Hydroponic Lettuce
authors: Ping Li, Wangxiang Ding, Hantang Wang, Chuanfa Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This work experimentally investigated the effects of plasma-activated water (PAW), generated by a dielectric barrier discharge (DBD), on the growth characteristics of lettuce. PAW was prepared using a needle-plate DBD electrode configuration, applied for seed soaking and hydroponic cultivation. The physicochemical parameters of PAW were analyzed, and its influence on germination and growth performance of lettuce was examined, with a particular focus on the role of reactive species concentrations. The effects of discharge voltage on the physicochemical properties of PAW were determined, including pH, oxidation–reduction potential (ORP), electrical conductivity (EC), and the concentrations of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {H_{2}O_{2}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {NO_{2}^{-}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {NO_{3}^{-}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. In addition, the responses of lettuce to PAW prepared under different voltages were evaluated in terms of water uptake, plant height, root morphology, fresh weight, and chlorophyll content. Results indicated that with increasing discharge voltage, the pH of PAW decreased, while the ORP and EC increased markedly. Concentrations of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {H_{2}O_{2}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {NO_{3}^{-}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> exhibited linear increases, whereas <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\mathrm {NO_{2}^{-}}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> concentration increased initially and then declined. At the discharge voltage of 13 kV, seed water uptake and germination rate were notably enhanced. In addition, PAW prepared under appropriate discharge voltages significantly improved lettuce growth performance, including plant height (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {10.9}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), fresh weight (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {17.9}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), total root length (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {85}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), root surface area (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {193}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), root volume (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {238}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), root dry mass (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {235}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), and chlorophyll content (up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {18.7}\%$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), compared with the control. These findings demonstrate that PAW exerts a positive effect on lettuce germination and growth, providing both theoretical and practical guidance for the application of PAW in leafy vegetable cultivation.

title: Characterizing Tests in IoT Software: Practices, Challenges and Opportunities
authors: Rufeng Chen, Hengcheng Zhu, Wuqi Zhang, Zixu Zhou, Lili Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is experiencing rapid growth. Smart devices are emerging in smart homes and industrial applications, performing mission-critical tasks. Bugs in IoT software can lead to severe consequences. For example, a buggy smart lock can allow unauthorized access to a private property. Testing is a primary practice to expose software bugs and ensure software quality. However, little is known about how IoT software is tested. To bridge this gap, we conducted the first empirical study on test cases in open-source IoT software. Specifically, we evaluated the effectiveness of test cases in IoT software, explored the challenges inherent in testing IoT software, and analyzed the usage of mock objects. Our results indicate that while IoT software often contains a considerable number of tests, their effectiveness remains limited. We identified the primary challenges in testing IoT software as managing complex interactions with various external dependencies, such as other network-reliant IoT components, file systems, operating systems, and databases. We also observed that the use of mock objects in IoT software closely aligns with our identified testing challenges. This alignment demonstrates the potential of mocking as a solution to enhance test coverage and address the complexities of IoT software testing.

title: Spatial Dynamics of the Western Ghats Ecosystem in Kerala, India
authors: Shivakumar B R, Bhojaraja B E, Pallavi M, Sayed Mohsin Reza
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Data Engineering (AIDE)
abstract: The Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage site and one of the world’s eight hottest biodiversity hotspots, harbor exceptionally rich and ecologically significant flora. This study presents a spatial analysis of vegetation distribution across the Kerala segment of the Western Ghats using remote sensing, GIS techniques, and vegetation indices including NDVI, EVI, LAI, and FPAR. By integrating satellite-derived vegetation indices with key terrain factors, vegetation patterns were mapped and interpreted at the district scale. The results reveal strong associations between vegetation indices and terrain factors, delineating distinct ecological zones ranging from lowland evergreen foresits to high-altitude shola–grassland mosaics. Notably, the Western Ghats cover 86.52% of Kerala’s state boundary, emphasizing their dominant influence on the state’s spatial and ecological framework. The findings provide an essential baseline for conservation planning, ecosystem management, and sustainable development in this biodiversity-rich and ecologically sensitive region.

title: TRAN: Texture Response Adjustment Network for Lightweight Single Image Super-Resolution
authors: Ping Zheng, Rongfeng Zhou, He Jiang, Wen Cui, Liangliang Chen, Qiqi Kou, Deqiang Cheng, Guangtao Zhai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR) aims to reconstruct a high-resolution (HR) image from a given low-resolution (LR) input. Reconstruction difficulty varies across textures of different complexity. However, existing methods apply a single, unified feature modeling and reconstruction pipeline for all textures. This often exacerbates the reconstruction imbalance across textures of varying complexity. To address this issue, this study proposes Texture Response Adjustment Network for SISR (TRAN). This method achieves more balanced fine-grained reconstruction and improves overall reconstruction quality by adaptively adjusting texture response. TRAN comprises two main components. The first mitigates local texture response bias within an image by decoupling textures of different complexity in the frequency domain for independent modeling and response adjustment. The second alleviates global texture response bias across different images by dynamically perceiving overall image complexity and adjusting the network structure accordingly. Experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed TRAN balances fine-grained reconstruction across textures of different complexity and improve overall reconstruction performance. Notably, on the ×4 SISR task of the Manga109 dataset, TRAN outperforms MambaIRv2-L by 0.31 dB in PSNR while reducing FLOPs by 38.1G.

title: RAPNet: Rate-Distortion-Aware Video Pre-Processing via One-Step Reinforcement Learning for Video Coding
authors: Yanchen Zhao, Jiaye Fu, Zhimeng Huang, Jiaqi Zhang, Siwei Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: In recent years, with the rapid development of Neural Network-based (NN-based) deep learning algorithms, NN-based video coding tools have been emerging, demonstrating excellent coding performance. However, constrained by limited computational power at the edge or on end-user devices, deploying high-performance NN-based coding tools is a challenging task. This limitation hinders the standardization and widespread application of NN-based coding tools. Recently, NN-based video pre-processing has gained significant attention from both industry and academia. These algorithms are deployed on the cloud and require no modifications to the video codec. Traditional pre-processing methods typically rely on handcrafted designs or natural degradation modeling for quality enhancement. These approaches cannot be jointly optimized with the encoder, making it difficult to perceive the encoding process or adapt to different video content. Moreover, the non-differentiable nature of real codecs makes conventional supervised optimization difficult. Therefore, we propose a one-step reinforcement learning-based framework for video pre-processing model training. We also develop a video pre-processing network capable of supporting 4K@60fps live streaming applications. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves significant performance improvements across various video codecs. Compared with codecs such as x265, VVenC, and SVT-AVS3, our method achieves more than a 20% BD-rate (VMAF) improvement. The inference code and pretrained model will be released at: https://github.com/pkuyczhao/RAPNet.

title: Decoupling Control of a Multi-segment Hybrid-Actuated Soft Origami Continuum Robot Through Variable Stiffness
authors: Jian Tao, Tianheng Li, Xiang Yao, Changlin Chen, Qiqiang Hu, Erbao Dong, Yang Zhao, Shiwu Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Soft origami continuum robots have attracted considerable attention because of their flexibility and adaptability, but they face challenges in control accuracy. This study presents a novel design and control scheme for large-deformation hybrid-actuated soft origami continuum robots to improve their motion performance in real tasks. An origami-based pneumatic chamber is used as the robot backbone to achieve a high extension ratio, and the hybrid variable stiffness principle combining antagonistic actuation and layer jamming further enhances the robot's overall structural stiffness. The robot performs precise movements by controlling tendons distributed in an external origami. An iterative training strategy based on long short-term memory is employed to model the inverse kinematics of the robot in consideration of the inherent hysteresis of origami robots. Step size features are introduced to improve model accuracy with limited data. The capability for variable stiffness enables the migration of the training model on the basis of a single segment, the accuracy of which is close to the submillimeter level. Decoupling control of each segment for a rear-driven multi-segment origami continuum robot is also achieved. Experiment results reveal that the trajectory tracking errors for single-segment and multi-segment of the robot are 1.58, and 2.81 mm, respectively, with relative errors of 0.71% and 0.63% over the robot length, demonstrating the good performance of the proposed design and control method. Orientation data can also be added to the dataset to achieve orientation control of the robot, with an angle error of 0.75°. The robot shows its stability and safety in a series of real tasks, including writing, LED tracking, board cleaning, and pick and place. This study demonstrates a potential solution for continuum robots performing precise tasks without any sensory feedback in confined spaces and specialized environments where electronic sensors fail.

title: Machine Learning-Enhanced Domain Decomposition FDTD for Electromagnetic Scattering Problems
authors: Wen-Hao Su, Wei Shao, Haiyan Ou, Li-Ye Xiao
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: An extreme learning machine (ELM)-enhanced domain decomposition finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method is proposed for electromagnetic scattering problems. It addresses the convergence challenge of standalone ELM-based methods at perfect electric conductor boundaries by decomposing the computational domain into three subdomains. Laguerre-FDTD is adopted for scatterer adjacent and perfectly matched layer regions, while ELM-based Laguerre FDTD is used for wave propagation regions. C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sup> and C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup> continuity conditions are employed to ensure field continuity and wave propagation consistency, and a two-dimensional numerical example verify the high accuracy and efficiency of the proposed method.

title: Enhancing Network Resilience: High Efficient Deployment Based on Digital Twin for Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Cangzhu Xu, Shanshan Song, Anfeng Li, Jun Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Deployment critically affects data transmission and monitoring in Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs). Static deployment provides the foundation for initial network connectivity and performance, whereas dynamic deployment ensures long-term resilience by adapting to environmental variations and node displacement. However, existing static schemes neglect acoustic ray bending due to underwater environmental inhomogeneity, which degrades topology optimization and transmission reliability. Meanwhile, dynamic approaches rely on reactive fault recovery without predictive mechanisms or performance validation, resulting in delayed or inefficient repairs. To address these limitations, we propose a High Efficient Deployment based on Digital Twin (HEDT) for enhancing resilience in UWSNs. HEDT integrates a reliability model that consider environment, acoustic propagation and topology to guide initial deployment. Then a Digital Twin Network (DTN) is constructed to mirror the physical UWSN, enabling fault prediction, performance monitoring, and deployment validation. To handle potential faults detected by DTN, HEDT generates dynamic deployment schemes through three reliability model-based strategies (low mobility cost, link relay enhancement, and load balance optimization). The optimal scheme is iteratively validated within the DTN and then deployed to reposition the physical nodes, achieving autonomous and efficient network recovery. Experimental results demon strate that HEDT outperforms existing deployment algorithms, achieving an average reliability improvement of 1.77%. Field experiments conducted in Danjiangkou Lake further validate its effectiveness in enabling a resilient underwater network.

title: Secure Agentic AI–Driven Semantic Communication Framework for Autonomous Vehicle Coordination in 6G Edge Networks
authors: Vishal Bharadwaj Meruga, Rahul Kumar Konduru, Sreenivas Reddy Sagili, Krushived Potturi, Bhaskar Gautam, Kunvar Chokshi, Deepak Gupta
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: The next-generation 6G networks are realized through Semantic communication as a tool of exchange between machines to ensure improved communication efficiency and intelligence as they share meanings, as opposed to raw data. At the same time, the concept of Agentic AI brings autonomous decision-making, based on contextual awareness, to coordinate complex systems (autonomous vehicles). Nonetheless, the combination of semantic communication and agent-based coordination pose serious issues regarding security, privacy and trust especially because of the information leakage and manipulation by adversaries. This paper presents a safe Agentic AI-based semantic communications system of coordinating autonomous vehicles in 6G edge networks. The framework has semantic feature extraction, agent-based decision making and edge-based intelligence, as well as a semantic level security mechanism to promote privacy-preserving and trustful interactions. The multi-objective approach of optimizing the latency, semantic accuracy and security is used. Findings of simulation indicate that the proposed method significantly reduces the latency (21.8%) and enhances the semantic accuracy (17.6%) and attack resilience (24.3%) in comparison to base methods. These findings indicate the suitability of the suggested structure in permitting safe and efficient coordination of autonomous car systems within the 6G surroundings.

title: Mario di Bernardo [People in Control]
authors: Mario di Bernardo
publish: IEEE Control Systems

title: Adaptive Current Control for High-Efficiency Motor Drive Operation in Transients
authors: Anton Dianov
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: To ensure efficient operation of synchronous motors (SMs), it is essential to apply techniques that minimize losses. In most control systems, the maximum torque per ampere (MTPA) control strategy is commonly used, as it offers a balance between simplicity and loss reduction. Developers typically focus on MTPA control during steady-state operation, which is the primary mode of most motor drives. However, in applications such as automotive systems and machine tools, motors often experience frequent transients, making it vital to incorporate MTPA control during these transients for optimal performance. This paper presents an algorithm designed to optimize current transients, aiming to guide the motor's current trajectory closer to the MTPA curve. Furthermore, considering that the Joule losses are proportional to squared currents, the proposed algorithm uses non-linear approximation, where approximation gains are proportional to squared currents. While the technique is inactive during steady-state operation, it becomes active when the control system detects a significant change in the current command and it dynamically modifies the gains of current controllers. When the transient is completed, the algorithm restores the original gains and deactivates. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed approach and show improved motor trajectories during transient operations.

authors: N/A

title: Tensorized Noise-aware Anchor Graph Learning with Deep Complementary Information Propagation for Scalable Multi-view Clustering
authors: Jian-Sheng Wu, Sang-Qi Zhao, Weidong Min, Wei-Shi Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Given the inherent consistency and diversity, multi-view data clustering requires leveraging complementary information and cross-view consistency. However, existing anchor graph-based methods for processing large-scale multi-view data exhibit three critical limitations: inability to fully exploit complementary information, insufficient exploration of cross-view consistency, and vulnerability to noise. To address these issues, we propose a novel model, Tensorized Noise-aware Anchor Graph Learning with Deep Complementary Information Propagation (TNAGL), for scalable multi-view clustering. Specifically, TNAGL introduces an autoencoder-like deep tensor factorization framework to derive a low-rank anchor graph tensor, which is designed to integrate view-specific anchor graphs, thus enabling the model to extract deep complementary information and cross-view consistency while suppressing noise. The framework hierarchically encodes the input anchor graph tensor into a latent tensor and subsequently reconstructs a denoised anchor graph tensor, by regularizing the residual tensor with the tensor <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$l_{2,1}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-norm to mitigate noise. During encoding and decoding, complementary information is hierarchically explored, fused, and propagated via tensor t-product, which performs circular multiplication between the frontal slices of two given tensors, to capture inter-view dependencies. Finally, the denoised anchor graph tensor is regularized by the tensor Schatten-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math></inline-formula> norm to promote a low-rank structure, thereby preserving high-order cross-view consistency. Experiments on real-world datasets, including a noise-corrupted one, consistently demonstrate the superiority of TNAGL.

title: Defense Strategy for Active Vehicle Suspension Systems with Incomplete Modes: Risk-Driven Gain-Scheduling and Experimental Validation
authors: Ying Wei, Xiangpeng Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In the open network environment of intelligent connected vehicles, both driving safety and ride comfort are threatened by cyber attacks. This paper focuses on the resilient defense strategy for active vehicle suspension systems (AVSS) against malicious attacks. To address the limitation that constructing a precise attack model is overly idealized, an incomplete Markov attack model is designed. This model accommodates uncertain transition probabilities, which are reasonably reconstructed via a probability fusion method, and is compatible with scenarios involving insufficient attack knowledge. More importantly, a risk-driven gain-scheduling mechanism is proposed under the polynomial fuzzy framework, where multiple operating modes are cooperatively switched based on real-time membership degree structures and risk severity. Specifically, a refined scheme assisted by the dynamic slack method is proposed to counteract compound disturbances from concurrent road excitations and attacks in risk system mode. By contrast, a simplified scheme is adopted to avoid the additional overhead caused by unnecessary switching in safe system mode. Finally, hardware-in-the-loop experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method.

title: CognNet: An Interpretable LLM-as-Judge Framework for SOLO-Level Prediction Using Structured Cognitive Evidence
authors: Jiayuan Liu, Xin Xing, Miao He, Feng Gao, Qipin Cheng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Automated cognitive-level prediction remains technically challenging because current models often lack interpretable alignment between language-model reasoning and theory-grounded evidence. We propose CognNet, an interpretable and locally deployable LLM-as-Judge framework that combines an instruction-fine-tuned Qwen3 text encoder, a TabNet branch over four automatically computed structured cognitive indicators, and a per-instance gated fusion module. On 1,847 expert-annotated Chinese middle school physics responses, CognNet achieves 0.78 accuracy and 0.76 Macro-F1 under student-grouped five-fold cross-validation, and 0.81 accuracy and 0.79 Macro-F1 under an optimistic instance-level split. The student-grouped result is used as the deployment-oriented estimate, while the instance-level result shows the upper-bound gain from integrating text semantics with structured cognitive evidence. Results indicate that explicit evidence fusion improves model reliability, evidence consistency, and transparent decision support for SOLO-level classification.

title: Knowledge Graph Neural Network Enabled Personalized and Efficient Content Caching for Large-Scale Social Networks
authors: Yaxu Wang, Peng Yu, Honglin Fang, Can Tan, Xinxiu Liu, Wenjing Li, Zhaowei Qu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: At present, most edge servers adopt popularity-based caching strategies, prioritizing the caching of content with the highest overall popularity on user-side edge servers. However, in social network scenarios, user interests and preferences are highly personalized and dynamically changing. This results in existing caching strategies often failing to adjust the cache placement of content in real time according to individual user preferences, leading to suboptimal edge cache hit rates, increased user request response latency, and a decline in quality of service (QoS) for the user experience. To address this issue, we propose a new caching strategy tailored for large-scale social content based on knowledge graph neural network (KGNNC). First, an entity-relation KG is constructed from users' triple data <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$(\boldsymbol{h}, \boldsymbol{r}, \boldsymbol{t})$</tex-math> </inline-formula> on social platforms. Next, a graph convolutional neural network is employed to iteratively aggregate feature information from neighboring nodes and learn vector representations of the nodes. Finally, a reinforcement learning-based algorithm is utilized to determine the optimal caching location for content. Experimental results on multiple public datasets demonstrate, compared with several existing baseline algorithms (least recently used, least frequently used, neural network-based collaborative filtering, KG-DQN, and CAFR), the algorithm proposed in this article achieves a reduction in the average response latency of requests by 38.26%, 34.46%, 13.56%, 6.49%, 4.19% on MovieLens 1M dataset and 30.31%, 27.59%, 14.11%, 5.83%, 4.78% on last FM dataset, respectively. Meanwhile, experiment results demonstrate that the caching hit rate is increased by 24.2%, 25.1%, 14.4%, 6.5%, 3.53% on MovieLens 1M and 39.51%, 39.05%, 23.40%, 10.66%, 8.67% on last FM compared with the four existing baseline algorithms, respectively. These results verify the effectiveness of our algorithm in reducing response latency of user requests and improving caching hit rate of edge servers in social networks.

title: Power Factor Analysis and Optimization Control of a DC Biased Hybrid Excitation Vernier Machine
authors: Liang Xu, Chen Qian, Tingting Jiang, Wenxiang Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This paper investigates power factor analysis and optimization control of the DC-biased hybrid excitation vernier machine. The machine has two hybrid excitation sources, viz. permanent magnet and zero-sequence current in virtual field winding. Different from conventional power factor optimization control derived in dq-axis without considering 0-axis, this paper aims to improve the power factor by fully utilizing additional control flexibility of zero-sequence current. An analytical model of the DCB-HEVM is established in the synchronous rotating reference frame, which captures the torque production and power characteristics. Based on this model, the influence of the three-dimensional dq0-axis current components on power factor is investigated, thereby clarifying the inherent coupling between torque generation and power factor regulation. Then, a power factor optimization control strategy based on coordinated dq0-axis current distribution is proposed. Experimental results show that with the proposed control strategy, the power factor can be effectively improved.

authors: N/A
